title: ROC curve — ROCCurve
text: Key features: Multiple classifiers — compare several prediction scores side--side providing multiple score_by columns. AUC display — area curve values shown plot legend, configurable precision. Optimal cutoffs — identify annotate optimal cutoff points using 30+ methods OptimalCutpoints package, supply custom numeric thresholds. Confidence intervals — add ROC confidence bands via plotROC::geom_rocci(). Axis orientation — reverse x-axis show specificity display axes percentages. Splitting faceting — split data sub-plots via split_by facet within single plot via facet_by.

title: ROC curve — ROCCurve
text: split_by provided, following pipeline executes: Validation — validate_common_args() checks random seed facet_by configuration. Column resolution — check_columns() resolves split_by (force_factor, allow_multi, concat_multi). Data splitting — Unused factor levels split_by dropped via droplevels(), data split split_by levels (preserving factor level order). split_by NULL, data wrapped single-element list named "...". Per-split resolution — check_palette(), check_palcolor(), check_legend() resolve per-split palette, colour, legend.position, legend.direction overrides. Per-split dispatch — split: Title resolution: title function, receives split level name; otherwise title %||% split_level used. split_by column removed per-split data frame avoid conflicts ROC analysis. ROCCurveAtomic() called per-split palette, palcolor, legend.position, legend.direction. Combination — combine_plots() assembles list plots via patchwork::wrap_plots, honouring nrow/ncol/byrow/design. AUC / cutoff collection — combine = TRUE, per-split auc cutoffs attributes collected combined data frames split_by column identifying source split, stored attr(p, "auc") attr(p, "cutoffs").

title: ROC curve — ROCCurve
code: set.seed(8525)  D.ex <- rbinom(200, size = 1, prob = .5) M1 <- rnorm(200, mean = D.ex, sd = .65) M2 <- rnorm(200, mean = D.ex, sd = 1.5) gender <- c("Male", "Female")[rbinom(200, 1, .49) + 1]  data <- data.frame(D = D.ex, D.str = c("Healthy", "Ill")[D.ex + 1],   gender = gender, M1 = M1, M2 = M2)  # --- Basic ROC curve --- ROCCurve(data, truth_by = "D", score_by = "M1")   # --- Will warn about the positive label --- ROCCurve(data, truth_by = "D.str", score_by = "M1") #> Warning: 'pos_label' is NULL, value 'Ill' from 'D.str' will be used as the positive label.   # --- Decreasing direction --- ROCCurve(data, truth_by = "D", score_by = "M1", increasing = FALSE)   # --- Multiple ROC curves (multiple classifiers) --- ROCCurve(data, truth_by = "D", score_by = c("M1", "M2"), group_name = "Method")   # --- Grouping by a column --- ROCCurve(data, truth_by = "D", score_by = "M1", group_by = "gender", show_auc = "plot")   # --- Reverse x-axis and display as percentages --- ROCCurve(data, truth_by = "D", score_by = "M1", x_axis_reverse = TRUE, percent = TRUE)   # --- Custom n_cuts and single colour --- ROCCurve(data, truth_by = "D", score_by = "M1", n_cuts = 10, palcolor = "black")   # --- Add confidence intervals --- ROCCurve(data, truth_by = "D", score_by = "M1", ci = list(sig.level = .01))   # --- Facet by a column --- ROCCurve(data, truth_by = "D", score_by = "M1", facet_by = "gender")   # --- Show cutoffs --- ROCCurve(data, truth_by = "D", score_by = "M1", cutoffs_at = c(0, "ROC01", "SpEqualSe"))   # --- Split by a column --- p <- ROCCurve(data, truth_by = "D", score_by = "M1", split_by = "gender",    cutoffs_at = c(0.2, "MaxSpSe")) p  # Retrieve the AUC values attr(p, "auc") #>   PANEL COORD group       AUC gender #> 1     1     1     1 0.8779167 Female #> 2     1     1     1 0.9039616   Male # Retrieve the cutoffs attr(p, "cutoffs") #>              cutoff         x         y              label ..group specificity #> 1               0.2 0.2833333 0.9250000              0.200           0.7166667 #> 2 0.359521377967435 0.2333333 0.8000000 0.360 (by MaxSpSe)           0.7666667 #> 3               0.2 0.3265306 0.8823529              0.200           0.6734694 #> 4 0.344386035694676 0.2040816 0.8235294 0.344 (by MaxSpSe)           0.7959184 #>   sensitivity gender #> 1   0.9250000 Female #> 2   0.8000000 Female #> 3   0.8823529   Male #> 4   0.8235294   Male

text: ggplot object height width attributes (inches) attached, plus attr(p, "auc") attr(p, "cutoffs") data frames.

title: Atomic ROC curve (internal) — ROCCurveAtomic
text: function produces ROC curve using plotROC::geom_roc(), following capabilities: Multiple classifiers — score_by accepts multiple column names, automatically pivoting grouped format several prediction scores can compared single plot. AUC calculation — area curve computed via plotROC::calc_auc() displayed either plot legend, controlled show_auc. Cutoff annotation — user-specified cutoffs (numeric score thresholds named optimal-cutoff methods OptimalCutpoints package) rendered markers labels, using ggrepel::geom_text_repel() label placement. Confidence intervals — optional ROC confidence bands via plotROC::geom_rocci(). Axis flexibility — supports reversed x-axis (displaying specificity) percent-scaled axes.

title: Atomic ROC curve (internal) — ROCCurveAtomic
text: show_auc resolution — match.arg() resolves show_auc one "auto", "none", "legend", "plot". Column validation — check_columns() validates truth_by (single column), score_by (multiple allowed), group_by (factor, multi-column concatenated). error raised group_by provided alongside multiple score_by columns. Positive label encoding — Converts truth_by binary numeric (0/1) three paths: pos_label provided: re-factor pos_label last level, convert. truth_by factor: warn last level treated positive, convert. Non-numeric, non-factor: coerce factor, warn, convert. Multi-score_by expansion — score_by contains multiple columns, tidyr::pivot_longer() reshapes single .score column .group identifier, becomes group_by variable. ggplot dispatch — Selects gglogger::ggplot ggplot2::ggplot based getOption("plotthis.gglogger.enabled"). Dummy group insertion — group_by = NULL, creates synthetic ..group column (constant "") curve still renders. legend suppressed ("none"). Auto AUC placement — show_auc = "auto", single-group faceted plots place AUC plot; multi-group plots place legend. Base ROC geometry — plotROC::geom_roc() aes(d = truth, m = score, color = group), controlling direction via increasing parameter. AUC calculation — Temporarily facets via facet_plot(), computes per-group (per-facet) AUC values via plotROC::calc_auc(). Cutoff computation — get_cutoffs_data() combines group_by facet_by columns .cat identifier computes per-category cutoff data, supporting numeric thresholds named OptimalCutpoints methods. Cutoff rendering — cutoff data non-NULL, splits .cat column back group/facet columns adds geom_point() (markers) geom_text_repel() (labels) configurable size, shape, stroke, colour, background styling. Confidence intervals — ci non-NULL, plotROC::geom_rocci() added provided arguments. AUC display — Three modes: "plot": geom_text() places AUC labels corner position determined increasing x_axis_reverse. "legend": AUC values appended scale_color_manual() labels (per-facet prefixes faceting active). "none": group level names used -. Diagonal reference — geom_abline() draws -discrimination line (y = x, y = -x x-axis reversed) dashed grey line. Color scale — scale_color_manual() assigns palette-derived colours via palette_this(), AUC-augmented labels show_auc = "legend". Axis formatting — Percent labels y-axis (x-axis) percent = TRUE. X-axis reversed (1 0) x_axis_reverse = TRUE, changing axis label "Specificity". Labels theme — labs() sets title, subtitle, x, y labels. theme, aspect ratio, legend position/direction, dashed grid lines applied. Dimension calculation — calculate_plot_dimensions() computes height width attributes base_height = 4.5, aspect ratio, legend metrics. Attribute storage — auc cutoffs data frames stored attr(p, "auc") attr(p, "cutoffs") retrieval exported wrapper. Faceting — facet_plot() wraps plot facet_wrap / facet_grid facet_by provided.

title: Atomic radar/spider plot (internal) — RadarPlotAtomic
text: Core implementation drawing single radar (spider) chart.  workhorse behind exported RadarPlot SpiderPlot functions — takes single data frame (split_by support) returns ggplot object. Radar charts display multivariate data two-dimensional polar coordinate system variable (x-axis category) placed along radial axis evenly spaced around circle.  data values connected lines forming polygon (multiple polygons group_by provided).  polygon = FALSE (default) grid rendered concentric circles (classic radar chart); polygon = TRUE straight polygonal grid lines used (spider chart variant). polar coordinate system implemented via local coord_radar() helper extends ggplot2::CoordPolar is_linear = TRUE, allowing discrete x-axis positions mapped evenly spaced angular coordinates.

title: Atomic radar/spider plot (internal) — RadarPlotAtomic
code: RadarPlotAtomic(   data,   x,   x_sep = "_",   group_by = NULL,   group_by_sep = "_",   y = NULL,   group_name = NULL,   groups = NULL,   scale_y = c("group", "global", "x", "none"),   y_min = 0,   y_max = NULL,   y_nbreaks = 4,   polygon = FALSE,   fill = TRUE,   linewidth = 1,   pt_size = 4,   max_charwidth = 16,   bg_color = "grey80",   bg_alpha = 0.1,   theme = "theme_this",   theme_args = list(),   palette = "Paired",   palcolor = NULL,   palreverse = FALSE,   facet_by = NULL,   facet_scales = "fixed",   facet_ncol = NULL,   facet_nrow = NULL,   facet_byrow = TRUE,   alpha = 0.2,   aspect.ratio = 1,   legend.position = waiver(),   legend.direction = "vertical",   keep_na = FALSE,   keep_empty = FALSE,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   ... )

title: Atomic radar/spider plot (internal) — RadarPlotAtomic
text: data data frame. x character string specifying column name data frame plot x-axis (radial categories).  Must character factor.  Multiple columns can provided; concatenated x_sep separator. x_sep character string used join multiple x columns.  Default "_".  Ignored x single column. group_by character vector column names group data separate filled polygons.  unique combination becomes distinct polygon layer.  Multiple columns concatenated group_by_sep.  NULL, single polygon drawn legend. group_by_sep character string used join multiple group_by columns.  Default "_". y character string specifying numeric column radial axis.  NULL, count observations (x, group_by, facet_by) combination used. group_name character string used colour/fill legend title.  NULL, group_by column name used. groups character vector group values (group_by column) include plot.  NULL, groups included.  can control groups appear legend order.  Implies keep_empty = FALSE group_by column: groups present data shown legend. scale_y radial axis scaled?  Default "group".  Options "group", "global", "x", "none". "group" — scaled fraction within group. "global" — scaled fraction total. "x" — scaled fraction within x-axis category. "none" — raw counts values, scaling. y_min numeric value setting minimum radial axis.  Default 0. y_max numeric value setting maximum radial axis.  NULL, maximum data value used. y_nbreaks numeric value number breaks (concentric grid lines) radial axis.  Default 4. polygon logical value. title: Atomic radar/spider plot (internal) — RadarPlotAtomic
text: Column resolution — x, y, group_by, facet_by validated transformed via check_columns.  Multi-column inputs x group_by concatenated using x_sep group_by_sep. Group setup — group_by NULL, dummy .group factor created polygons still draw.  legend suppressed.  groups provided, data filtered group levels. Count aggregation — y = NULL, count observations unique (x, group_by, facet_by) combination computed.  Factor levels preserved aggregation. Proportion scaling — scale_y argument controls y-value normalisation: "group" scales within group, "global" within facet (whole dataset), "x" within x category, "none" leaves values raw.  Percent labels used scaled modes. NA / empty-level handling — process_keep_na_empty() applies keep_na keep_empty policies.  Per-column keep_empty settings x, group_by, facet_by extracted; facet columns must share value. Coordinate setup — local coord_radar() creates CoordPolar subclass is_linear = TRUE, placing discrete x positions evenly spaced angles. Y-axis range — y_min, y_max, y_nbreaks determine radial axis limits number concentric grid lines. Colour mapping — palette_this() assigns colours group_by levels, including NA (defaulting "grey80"). Background layer — polygon = TRUE, polygonal background fills area y_min y_max.  polygon = FALSE, smooth circular background interpolated via 360 sample points. Radial grid lines — geom_path() draws radial lines y_min y_max x-axis position, respecting facets. Polygon grid — polygon = TRUE, concentric polygonal grid lines drawn break level via geom_polygon(). Data rendering — geom_polygon() draws filled (unfilled) polygons group. geom_point() adds points observation. Radial axis labels rendered text fixed angular offset. Scale configuration — scale_y_continuous() sets radial axis limits breaks. scale_x_discrete() positions category labels. Colour scales use scale_fill_manual() scale_color_manual() drop controlled keep_empty_group. Circular grid lines — polygon = FALSE, dashed circular grid lines styled via panel.grid.major.y. Dimension calculation — calculate_plot_dimensions() computes plot height width aspect.ratio, legend metrics, base height. Faceting — facet_plot() wraps plot facet_wrap / facet_grid facet_by supplied, respecting keep_empty setting facet variables.

title: Rarefaction / extrapolation plot — RarefactionPlot
text: Draws rarefaction extrapolation curves biodiversity data using iNEXT package.  Accepts raw species-abundance / incidence-frequency lists (passed iNEXT() estimation) pre-computed iNEXT objects. function supports three curve types (sample-size-based, sample completeness, coverage-based), diversity orders (q), per-group colouring, faceting, splitting separate sub-plots via split_by.  Observed data marked points, rarefaction lines solid, extrapolation segments dashed.  Confidence intervals shown semi-transparent ribbons.

title: Rarefaction / extrapolation plot — RarefactionPlot
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(8525) spider <- list(    Girdled = c(46, 22, 17, 15, 15, 9, 8, 6, 6, 4, rep(2, 4), rep(1, 12)),    Logged = c(88, 22, 16, 15, 13, 10, 8, 8, 7, 7, 7, 5, 4, 4, 4, 3, 3, 3, 3,      2, 2, 2, 2, rep(1, 14)) )  # Basic sample-size-based rarefaction (type = 1) RarefactionPlot(spider)   # Multiple diversity orders with faceting RarefactionPlot(spider, q = c(0, 1, 2), facet_by = "q")   # Multiple diversity orders split into sub-plots RarefactionPlot(spider, q = c(0, 1, 2), split_by = "q")   # Per-split palettes RarefactionPlot(spider, q = c(0, 1, 2), split_by = "q",                 palette = c("0" = "Paired", "1" = "Set1", "2" = "Dark2"))   # Coverage-based rarefaction (type = 3) with # group_by = "q" and facet_by = "group" RarefactionPlot(spider, q = c(0, 1, 2), group_by = "q",  facet_by = "group", palette = "Set1", type = 3)  # }

title: Atomic rarefaction / extrapolation plot (internal) — RarefactionPlotAtomic
text: Core implementation drawing single rarefaction (extrapolation) curve biodiversity data.  workhorse behind exported RarefactionPlot — takes single fortifed data frame (produced fortify() iNEXT object) returns ggplot object. function renders three types curves: Sample-size-based (type = 1) — species diversity function sample size (number individuals sampling units), rarefaction (interpolation) extrapolation segments. Sample completeness (type = 2) — sample coverage function sample size. Coverage-based (type = 3) — species diversity function sample coverage. Observed data points marked geom_point() (shape per group), rarefaction / extrapolation lines drawn geom_line() using solid/dashed linetype distinguish two phases, confidence intervals rendered semi-transparent ribbons se = TRUE fortifed data contains y.lwr / y.upr columns.

title: Atomic rarefaction / extrapolation plot (internal) — RarefactionPlotAtomic
text: ggplot dispatch — selects gglogger::ggplot ggplot2::ggplot based getOption("plotthis.gglogger.enabled"). Axis labels — set according combination type data's datatype attribute ("abundance" vs. "incidence"): type = 2: x = "Number individuals" "Number sampling units", y = "Sample coverage". type = 3 || 4: x = "Sample coverage", y = "Species diversity". type = 1 (abundance): x = "Number individuals", y = "Species diversity". type = 1 (incidence): x = "Number sampling units", y = "Species diversity". Group name fallback — group_name NULL, set value group_by (.e. "q" "group"). Base ggplot — initialises ggplot(data, aes(x, y,         color = group_by)). Observed data points — geom_point() rows Method == "Observed", shape mapped group variable. Colour scale — scale_color_manual() using palette_this().  legend suppressed group_by dummy ".group". Shape scale — scale_shape_discrete() legend-suppression logic. Rarefaction / extrapolation lines — geom_line() linetype mapped lty column ("Rarefaction" vs. "Extrapolation"). Linetype scale — scale_linetype_manual() solid dashed values, legend key width set 1 cm. Theme labels — do_call(theme, theme_args), panel.grid.major (grey80 dashed), aspect ratio, legend position / direction, title / subtitle. Confidence ribbon — se = TRUE, geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = y.lwr, ymax = y.upr, fill = group_by)) added per-group palette transparency alpha. Dimension calculation — calculate_plot_dimensions() computes height / width attributes base_height = 4.5, aspect.ratio, legend metrics. Faceting — facet_plot() wraps plot facet_wrap / facet_grid facet_by provided.

title: Ridge Plot — RidgePlot
text: Ridge (joy) plot visualising distribution numeric variable across multiple groups. group rendered partially overlapping density curve along y-axis, making easy compare distribution shapes, central tendency, spread across categories. function supports long wide data formats: Long form (in_form = "long", default) — numeric column (x) plus factor column (group_by) whose levels become y-axis ridges. Wide form (in_form = "wide") — multiple numeric columns listed group_by gathered internally long form. Optional vertical reference lines (add_vline) can mark group means, specific values, per-group thresholds. Supports faceting, split-splitting, full palette customisation.

title: Ridge Plot — RidgePlot
text: ggplot object (single plot), patchwork / wrap_plots object (split_by provided combine = TRUE), list ggplot objects (split_by provided combine = FALSE).

title: Ridge Plot — RidgePlot
text: split_by specified, RidgePlot() executes following pipeline: Argument validation — validate_common_args() checks seed facet-consistency. NA / empty normalisation — check_keep_na() / check_keep_empty() convert keep_na / keep_empty per-column lists. Theme resolution — process_theme() resolves theme string theme function. Split column resolution — check_columns() validates split_by (force_factor, concat_multi). Pre-filtering — process_keep_na_empty() removes NA / empty levels split column, data split split_by levels (order preserved). Per-split parameter resolution — check_palette(), check_palcolor(), check_legend() resolve palette, palcolor, legend.position, legend.direction split. Per-split dispatch — split passed RidgePlotAtomic() resolved parameters. Title defaults split level name unless title function (case called default). Combination — combine_plots() assembles list plots via patchwork::wrap_plots(), applying nrow, ncol, byrow, axes, axis_titles, guides, design.

title: Atomic ridge plot — RidgePlotAtomic
text: Core implementation ridge (joy) plots. Renders overlapping density curves group y-axis using ggridges::geom_density_ridges(), optional vertical reference lines wide--long data conversion. function accepts data two forms: long form (default) — numeric x column plus group_by factor column whose levels become y-axis ridges. wide form — multiple numeric columns named group_by gathered via tidyr::pivot_longer() .x / .group columns, processed identically long form. Vertical reference lines (add_vline) can specified numeric vector (lines groups), named list (per-group values), TRUE (group means). vline_color = TRUE, line coloured darkened blend corresponding ridge fill.

title: Atomic ridge plot — RidgePlotAtomic
code: RidgePlotAtomic(   data,   x = NULL,   in_form = c("long", "wide"),   group_by = NULL,   group_by_sep = "_",   group_name = NULL,   add_vline = NULL,   vline_type = "solid",   vline_color = TRUE,   vline_width = 0.5,   vline_alpha = 1,   flip = FALSE,   alpha = 0.8,   scale = NULL,   theme = "theme_this",   theme_args = list(),   palette = "Paired",   palcolor = NULL,   palreverse = FALSE,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   x_text_angle = 90,   keep_na = FALSE,   keep_empty = FALSE,   reverse = FALSE,   facet_by = NULL,   facet_scales = "fixed",   facet_ncol = NULL,   facet_nrow = NULL,   facet_byrow = TRUE,   aspect.ratio = 1,   legend.position = "none",   legend.direction = "vertical",   ... )

title: Atomic ridge plot — RidgePlotAtomic
text: data data frame. Accepted two forms: long (in_form = "long"): numeric column (named x) factor column (named group_by) whose levels become y-axis ridges. wide (in_form = "wide"): multiple numeric columns listed group_by gathered .x / .group via tidyr::pivot_longer(). x character string specifying column name numeric values plotted x-axis. in_form = "wide", x NULL; gathered values stored synthetic .x column. in_form character string specifying whether data "long" (default) "wide" format. group_by character string specifying column(s) whose levels define individual ridges y-axis. Multiple columns concatenated group_by_sep. wide mode, column names gather. group_by_sep character string used join multiple group_by column values single factor level. wide form columns concatenated (becomes ridge). Default: "_". group_name character string used legend title group_by fill aesthetic. Defaults (concatenated) group_by column name. add_vline specification vertical reference lines: NULL FALSE: lines. TRUE: draw line mean group. numeric vector: draw lines groups. named list numeric vectors: per-group lines, names match group_by levels. vline_type character string specifying line type vertical reference lines. Passed linetype geom_vline(). Default: "solid". vline_color colour vertical reference lines: literal colour value vector (recycled): applied directly. TRUE (default): line coloured darkened blend corresponding ridge fill colour, computed via blend_colors(mode = "multiply"). vline_width numeric value thickness vertical reference lines. Passed linewidth geom_vline(). Default: 0.5. vline_alpha numeric value [0, 1] transparency vertical reference lines. Default: 1. flip logical value. TRUE, axes swapped via coord_flip(). X-axis text angle grid-line placement adjusted accordingly. alpha numeric value [0, 1] transparency ridge fill. Default: 0.8. scale numeric value controlling vertical overlap ridges. Passed ggridges::geom_density_ridges(scale = ...). Smaller values increase overlap. NULL, ggridges auto-computes scale. theme character string theme class (.e. ggplot2::theme_classic) specifying theme use. Default "theme_this". theme_args list arguments pass theme function. palette character string specifying palette use. named list vector can used specify palettes different split_by values. palcolor character string specifying color use palette. named list can used specify colors different split_by values. values missing, values palette used (palcolor NULL values). palreverse logical value indicating whether reverse palette. Default FALSE. title character string specifying title plot. function can used generate title based default title. useful split_by used title needs dynamic. subtitle character string specifying subtitle plot. xlab character string specifying x-axis label. ylab character string specifying y-axis label. x_text_angle numeric value specifying angle (degrees) x-axis text flip = TRUE. Used calc_just() compute optimal hjust / vjust. Default: 90. keep_na logical value character replace NA values data. can also take named list specify different behavior different columns. TRUE NA, NA values replaced NA. FALSE, NA values removed data plotting. character string provided, NA values replaced provided string. named vector/list provided, names column names apply behavior , values one TRUE, FALSE, character string. Without named vector/list, behavior applies categorical/character columns used plot, example, x, group_by, fill_by, etc. keep_empty One FALSE, TRUE "level". can also take named list specify different behavior different columns. Without named list, behavior applies categorical/character columns used plot, example, x, group_by, fill_by, etc. FALSE (default): Drop empty factor levels data plotting. TRUE: Keep empty factor levels show separate category plot. "level": Keep empty factor levels, show plot. assigned colors palette maintain consistency across multiple plots. Alias: levels reverse logical value. TRUE, y-axis group order reversed. NA groups renamed literal string "NA" placed end. facet_by character string specifying column name data frame facet plot. Otherwise, data split split_by generate multiple plots combine one using patchwork::wrap_plots facet_scales Whether scale axes facets. Default "fixed" options "free", "free_x", "free_y". See ggplot2::facet_wrap facet_ncol numeric value specifying number columns facet. facet_by single column facet_wrap used. facet_nrow numeric value specifying number rows facet. facet_by single column facet_wrap used. facet_byrow logical value indicating whether fill plots row. Default TRUE. aspect.ratio numeric value specifying aspect ratio plot. legend.position character string specifying position legend. waiver(), single groups, legend "none", otherwise "right". legend.direction character string specifying direction legend. ... Additional arguments passed ggridges::geom_density_ridges() (bandwidth, jittered_points, quantile_lines, etc.).

title: Atomic ridge plot — RidgePlotAtomic
text: RidgePlotAtomic executes following steps: ggplot dispatch — selects gglogger::ggplot ggplot2::ggplot. Wide--long conversion — in_form = "wide", calls tidyr::pivot_longer() group_by columns, producing .group (factor) .x (values). x group_by redirected synthetic columns. Column resolution — check_columns() validates x, group_by (force_factor, allow_multi, concat_multi), facet_by. Default group — group_by NULL, synthetic .group factor single space character level created fill pipeline runs uniformly. Reverse ordering — reverse = TRUE, factor levels group_by reversed, flipping y-axis ridge order. NA / empty handling — process_keep_na_empty() filters data. reverse = TRUE group value NA, NA level renamed literal string "NA" moved end factor colour mapping display remain consistent. Palette resolution — palette_this() maps group levels fill colours. Base ggplot — initialises ggplot(data, aes(x, y, fill)) group_by y-axis. Ridge geometry — ggridges::geom_density_ridges(alpha, scale). scale NULL, ggridges auto-computes overlap factor. Vertical reference lines — add_vline NULL / FALSE: add_vline = TRUE → computes tapply(x, group_by, mean). vline_color = TRUE → resolves per-group line colours darkening fill colour via blend_colors(mode = "multiply"). Named list elements matched factor levels. Adds geom_vline(xintercept, linetype, linewidth, color, alpha). Scales labels — scale_y_discrete(drop = !keep_empty_group), scale_x_continuous(), labs(). Fill scale — scale_fill_manual(). keep_empty_group = TRUE, drop = FALSE, breaks, limits set preserve empty factor levels. Flip-aware theme — flip = TRUE: coord_flip() applied. x-axis text angle set x_text_angle computed hjust / vjust via calc_just(). Major grid lines drawn x-axis. flip = FALSE, y-axis text right-aligned grid lines appear y-axis. Theme application — do_call(theme, theme_args) applies resolved theme function, aspect.ratio legend position set. Dimension calculation — calculate_plot_dimensions(base_height = 1, n_y = nlevels(group_by), y_scale_factor = 1, aspect.ratio, legend, flip) sets height / width attributes. base height 1 unit per ridge keeps individual ridges compact. Faceting — facet_plot() applies facet_grid / facet_wrap, drop = !keep_empty_facet.

title: Ring plot (multi-layer donut chart) — RingPlot
text: Draws ring plot (multi-layer donut chart) level x becomes concentric ring divided filled segments group_by. plot built geom_col() coord_polar("y"), producing publication-quality ring chart automatic count aggregation, per-group colour assignment, faceting, splitting sub-plots. x = NULL, single-ring plot produced (functionally equivalent pie chart via PieChart).

title: Ring plot (multi-layer donut chart) — RingPlot
code: # \donttest{ # Basic single-ring plot (pie-chart-like) RingPlot(datasets::iris, group_by = "Species")   # Multi-ring plot with faceting RingPlot(datasets::mtcars, x = "cyl", group_by = "carb", facet_by = "vs")   # Split into sub-plots with per-split palettes RingPlot(datasets::mtcars, x = "cyl", group_by = "carb", split_by = "vs",         palette = c("0" = "Set1", "1" = "Paired"))   # Custom data with NA and empty levels data <- data.frame(   x = factor(c("A", "B", NA, "D", "A", "B", NA, "D"), levels = c("A", "B", "C", "D")),   y = c(1, 2, 5, 3, 4, 5, 2, 6),   group = factor(c("a", "a", "a", NA, NA, "c", "c", "c"), levels = c("a", "b", "c")) )  # Default: NA and empty levels dropped RingPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", group_by = "group")   # Keep NA and empty levels RingPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", group_by = "group",         keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = TRUE)   # Per-column keep_na / keep_empty via named lists RingPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", group_by = "group",         keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = list(x = FALSE, group = 'level'))  # }

text: ggplot object height width attributes (inches) attached.

title: Atomic ring plot (internal) — RingPlotAtomic
text: Column resolution — x, group_by, y, facet_by validated transformed via check_columns.  x NULL, dummy .x column (value 1) created, producing single-ring (pie-chart-like) plot.  group_by NULL, dummy .group_by factor created legend suppressed. Multi-column group_by inputs concatenated group_by_sep. NA / empty-level handling — process_keep_na_empty() applies keep_na keep_empty policies.  Per-column keep_empty settings extracted x, group_by, facet_by independently.  facet columns must identical keep_empty values. Count aggregation — y = NULL, count observations unique (x, group_by, facet_by) combination computed new .y column. Factor levels preserved aggregation. Proportion scaling — y-values divided sum within (x, facet_by) group, normalising ring sum 1.0.  essential polar coordinates ring represents full 360 degrees. Ring resolution — levels x reversed outermost ring appears first data (rings drawn outward origin).  NA levels appended. Label logic — label = NULL, labels automatically suppressed single-ring plots shown multi-ring plots.  Labels placed inner edge ring via geom_label(). Clockwise ordering — clockwise = TRUE (default), group_by levels reversed segments appear clockwise top.  legend guide also reverses order. Colour mapping — palette_this() assigns colours group_by levels, including NA (defaulting "grey80"). Plot assembly — ggplot object built geom_col() (ring segments), coord_polar("y") (polar transform), scale_x_discrete() (leading space level donut hole), scale_fill_manual() per-group colours.  axes, ticks, grid lines, panel borders removed clean appearance.  fill scale drop argument controlled keep_empty_group. Dimension calculation — calculate_plot_dimensions() computes plot height width aspect.ratio legend metrics.  resulting height / width attributes stored ggplot object. Faceting — facet_plot() wraps plot facet_wrap / facet_grid facet_by provided, respecting keep_empty setting facet variables.

title: Atomic Sankey / alluvial diagram (internal) — SankeyPlotAtomic
text: Core implementation drawing Sankey (alluvial) diagram using geom_alluvium (geom_flow) geom_stratum.  function takes single data frame (split_by support) returns ggplot object faceting applied. function supports five input formats (in_form) control data columns interpreted.  Nodes (strata) rendered vertical blocks whose fill colour alpha can customised independently links (alluvia / flows) connecting .  Link colours can match node colours use separate palette; link borders can set fixed colour follow fill colour (links_color = ".fill"). Automatic legend resolution determines whether nodes different x-axis positions receive merged legend separate legends, based overlaps stratum values across positions.

title: Atomic Sankey / alluvial diagram (internal) — SankeyPlotAtomic
code: SankeyPlotAtomic(   data,   in_form = c("auto", "long", "lodes", "wide", "alluvia", "counts"),   x,   x_sep = "_",   y = NULL,   stratum = NULL,   stratum_sep = "_",   alluvium = NULL,   alluvium_sep = "_",   flow = FALSE,   nodes_color = "grey30",   links_fill_by = NULL,   links_fill_by_sep = "_",   links_name = NULL,   links_color = "gray80",   nodes_palette = "Paired",   nodes_palcolor = NULL,   palreverse = FALSE,   nodes_alpha = 1,   nodes_label = FALSE,   nodes_width = 0.25,   nodes_label_miny = 0,   nodes_legend = c("auto", "separate", "merge", "none"),   expand = c(0, 0, 0, 0),   links_palette = "Paired",   links_palcolor = NULL,   links_alpha = 0.6,   legend.box = "vertical",   keep_empty = TRUE,   x_text_angle = 0,   aspect.ratio = 1,   legend.position = "right",   legend.direction = "vertical",   flip = FALSE,   theme = "theme_this",   theme_args = list(),   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   facet_by = NULL,   facet_scales = "fixed",   facet_ncol = NULL,   facet_nrow = NULL,   facet_byrow = TRUE,   ... )

title: Atomic Sankey / alluvial diagram (internal) — SankeyPlotAtomic
text: data data frame. in_form character string specifying input data format. One "auto" (default), "long", "lodes", "wide", "alluvia", "counts". "long" alias "lodes"; "wide" alias "alluvia".  See data parameter SankeyPlot format descriptions. x character vector column name(s) x-axis categories. unique value concatenated pair represents time point, state, position along horizontal axis.  Behaviour depends in_form: "lodes" least one column expected; "alluvia" "counts" least two columns required.  latter two cases x_sep used. x_sep character string join multiple x columns in_form "lodes" auto-determined lodes. Default "_". y character string specifying numeric column y-axis (frequency / value).  NULL (default), count observations per combination x, stratum, alluvium, links_fill_by, facet_by computed automatically. Ignored in_form "counts". stratum character string specifying column defines node categories x-axis position.  unique value becomes stratum (node block) x position.  NULL, defaults links_fill_by.  Multiple columns concatenated stratum_sep.  Ignored "alluvia" format. stratum_sep character string join multiple stratum columns.  Default "_". alluvium character string specifying column identifies individual flows (alluvia) across x-axis positions.  unique value represents single observational unit tracked across positions.  NULL "counts" format, auto-generated identifier created.  Multiple columns concatenated alluvium_sep. Ignored "alluvia" format. alluvium_sep character string join multiple alluvium columns.  Default "_". flow logical value.  FALSE (default), geom_alluvium() used links.  TRUE, geom_flow() used instead, draws flows directional gradient x positions. nodes_color character string specifying border colour node (stratum) rectangles.  Use special value ".fill" match border colour node fill colour.  Default "grey30". links_fill_by character string specifying column determines fill colour links (alluvia / flows).  NULL "lodes" format, defaults alluvium.  "counts" format "." prefix, parameter required.  Multiple columns concatenated links_fill_by_sep. links_fill_by_sep character string join multiple links_fill_by columns.  Default "_". links_name character string legend title link fill scale.  NULL (default), links_fill_by column name used. links_color character string specifying border colour links (alluvia / flows).  Use special value ".fill" match link border colour link fill colour.  Default "gray80". nodes_palette character string specifying colour palette node (stratum) fill.  Passed palette_this(). Default "Paired". nodes_palcolor character vector custom colours node fill, used palcolor palette_this().  NULL (default), palette colours used directly. palreverse logical value indicating whether reverse palette. Default FALSE. nodes_alpha numeric value \([0, 1]\) controlling transparency node (stratum) fill.  Default 1. nodes_label logical value.  TRUE, stratum labels drawn inside node using geom_label() StatStratum.  Default FALSE. nodes_width numeric value (typically 0–1) specifying width node (stratum) rectangles fraction x-axis spacing. Default 0.25. nodes_label_miny numeric value specifying minimum y (frequency) threshold displaying node labels.  Nodes y-values threshold labelled.  Default 0. nodes_legend Controls node legend displayed.  One : "auto" (default) Automatically determined: nodes_label = TRUE, stratum identical links_fill_by matching colours, legend hidden. Otherwise, overlapping stratum values across x positions checked: overlap produces merged legend; overlap produces separate legends per x position. "merge" single merged legend nodes. "separate" One legend per x-axis position, generated via separate scale_fill_manual() layers. "none" node legend shown. expand values expand x y axes. like CSS padding. single value provided, used axes sides. two values provided, first value used top/bottom side second value used left/right side. three values provided, first value used top side, second value used left/right side, third value used bottom side. four values provided, values used top, right, bottom, left sides, respectively. can also use named vector specify values side. axis discrete, values applied 'add' 'expansion' function. axis continuous, values applied 'mult' 'expansion' function. See also https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/expansion.html links_palette character string specifying colour palette link fill.  Passed palette_this(). Default "Paired". links_palcolor character vector custom colours link fill, used palcolor palette_this().  NULL (default), palette colours used directly. links_alpha numeric value \([0, 1]\) controlling transparency link fill.  Default 0.6. legend.box character string specifying arrangement legend boxes, either "vertical" (default) "horizontal". keep_empty One FALSE, TRUE "level". can also take named list specify different behavior different columns. Without named list, behavior applies categorical/character columns used plot, example, x, group_by, fill_by, etc. FALSE (default): Drop empty factor levels data plotting. TRUE: Keep empty factor levels show separate category plot. "level": Keep empty factor levels, show plot. assigned colors palette maintain consistency across multiple plots. Alias: levels x_text_angle numeric value specifying angle x-axis text. aspect.ratio numeric value specifying aspect ratio plot. legend.position character string specifying position legend. waiver(), single groups, legend "none", otherwise "right". legend.direction character string specifying direction legend. flip logical value.  TRUE, coord_flip() applied swap x y axes. Default FALSE. theme character string theme class (.e. ggplot2::theme_classic) specifying theme use. Default "theme_this". theme_args list arguments pass theme function. title character string specifying title plot. function can used generate title based default title. useful split_by used title needs dynamic. subtitle character string specifying subtitle plot. xlab character string specifying x-axis label. ylab character string specifying y-axis label. facet_by character string specifying column name data frame facet plot. Otherwise, data split split_by generate multiple plots combine one using patchwork::wrap_plots facet_scales Whether scale axes facets. Default "fixed" options "free", "free_x", "free_y". See ggplot2::facet_wrap facet_ncol numeric value specifying number columns facet. facet_by single column facet_wrap used. facet_nrow numeric value specifying number rows facet. facet_by single column facet_wrap used. facet_byrow logical value indicating whether fill plots row. Default TRUE. ... Additional arguments passed geom_alluvium() geom_flow(), depending flow setting.  geom_flow distinct links_fill_by column, passing stat = "alluvium" preserves fill variable.

title: Atomic Sankey / alluvial diagram (internal) — SankeyPlotAtomic
text: Format resolution — in_form matched aliased ("long" → "lodes", "wide" → "alluvia"). Data parsing format — one five code paths executes: Lodes / long — x, stratum, alluvium, links_fill_by validated via check_columns().  Multi-column inputs concatenated respective separators.  y NULL, counts computed per (x, stratum, alluvium, links_fill_by, facet_by). Counts (x without "." prefix) — numeric x columns pivoted long form via pivot_longer(), creating y-axis cell values.  alluvium stratum default links_fill_by. Counts source node (x "." first element, is_flowcounts) — counts also injects links_fill_by values additional first column nodes, visualising flows originate source groups. Alluvia / wide — validated via is_alluvia_form() converted lodes form via to_lodes_form(). stratum alluvium ignored. Auto / fallback — branch matches, lodes path attempted validated via is_lodes_form(). Palette assignment — palette_this() resolves colours nodes (nodes_palette / nodes_palcolor) links (links_palette / links_palcolor). Flow-counts guide logic — is_flowcounts TRUE, links guide suppressed first-column node colours match link colours, avoiding redundant legends.  palettes identical colours differ (colours palette), first-column node colours reused. Legend auto-detection — nodes_legend = "auto": nodes_label = TRUE stratum links_fill_by share identical values colours, nodes legend hidden.  Otherwise, overlapping stratum values across x-axis positions checked: overlap triggers merged legend; overlap produces separate legends per position. Base ggplot — constructed aes(x = x,         stratum = stratum, alluvium = alluvium, y = y). Separate node fill layers — stratum differs links_fill_by nodes_legend = "separate", geom_col() + scale_fill_manual() layer pair added per x-axis position, followed new_scale_fill() produce independent legends. Link rendering — geom_alluvium() (default) geom_flow() flow = TRUE.  links_color = ".fill", colour aesthetic mapped links_fill_by colour scale guide suppressed.  geom_flow distinct stratum links_fill_by, stat = "alluvium" forced preserve fill variable flow stat transformation. Link fill scale — scale_fill_manual() links_colors resolved links_guide. Node rendering — geom_stratum() nodes_color (border) nodes_alpha.  nodes_color = ".fill", colour aesthetic maps stratum. Node fill scale — scale_fill_manual() nodes_colors.  guide "none" nodes_legend "none" "separate" (already handled per-position layers); otherwise "legend". Labels — nodes_label = TRUE, geom_label() stat = StatStratum min.y = nodes_label_miny. Axes, theme, labels — scale_x_discrete(), scale_y_continuous(), custom theme, plot title / subtitle, axis labels, aesthetic adjustments (aspect ratio, legend position / direction, grid removal, x-text angle). Coordinate flip — flip = TRUE, coord_flip() swaps axes. Dimension calculation — calculate_plot_dimensions() computes height width attributes number x-axis positions, legend metrics, flip state, scaled aspect.ratio. Faceting — facet_plot() wraps result facet_by provided.

title: Scatter Plot — ScatterPlot
text: Draws scatter plot optional size encoding, colour encoding (continuous gradient discrete palette), point highlighting, axis transformations. user-facing wrapper around ScatterPlotAtomic adds split_by support (generating separate sub-plots per group) combines via patchwork. Key features: Variable point size – size_by accepts either numeric constant column name. Colour modes – numeric color_by produces continuous gradient; factor/character color_by produces discrete palette. Colour scale trimming – lower_quantile / upper_quantile (explicit lower_cutoff / upper_cutoff) trim/clamp continuous colour scale extremes. Border modes – border_color can constant colour, TRUE (track fill gradient), omitted. Point highlighting – highlight accepts indices, rownames, logical TRUE, string expression. Axis transformation – xtrans / ytrans support log, sqrt, scale transformations. Split sub-plots – split_by produces one scatter plot per group level, combined single patchwork layout.

title: Scatter Plot — ScatterPlot
text: split_by provided: Seed validation – validate_common_args() sets random seed reproducibility. Theme resolution – process_theme() resolves theme string function. Split column resolution – check_columns() validates split_by (force_factor, allow_multi, concat_multi). Data splitting – unused factor levels dropped data split named list (preserving factor level order). split_by = NULL, single-element list named "..." used. Per-split palette / colour – check_palette() check_palcolor() resolve per-split palette colour overrides. Per-split legend – check_legend() resolves legend.position legend.direction per split. Per-split title – title function, receives default title (split level name) can return custom string; otherwise title %||% split_level used. Dispatch – split subset passed ScatterPlotAtomic(). Combination – combine_plots() assembles list plots via patchwork::wrap_plots, honouring nrow/ncol/byrow/design.

title: Scatter Plot — ScatterPlot
code: set.seed(8525)  data <- data.frame(    x = rnorm(20),    y = rnorm(20),    w = abs(rnorm(20)),    t = sample(c("A", "B"), 20, replace = TRUE) )  # --- Basic scatter plot --- ScatterPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y")   # --- Highlight points --- ScatterPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", highlight = 'x > 0')   # --- Size encoding (column name) --- ScatterPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", size_by = "w")   # --- Colour encoding (numeric gradient) --- ScatterPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", color_by = "w")   # --- Colour encoding (categorical) with border --- ScatterPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", size_by = "w", color_by = "t",  border_color = "red")   # --- Border colour tracks fill gradient --- ScatterPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", size_by = "w", color_by = "t",  border_color = TRUE)   # --- Shape without fill (single colour aesthetic) --- ScatterPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", size_by = "w", color_by = "t",  shape = 1, palette = "Set1")   # --- split_by with per-split palcolor --- ScatterPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", split_by = "t",             palcolor = list(A = "blue", B = "red"))   # --- Colour scale limits (quantile-based) --- ScatterPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", color_by = "w",             lower_quantile = 0.1, upper_quantile = 0.9)   # --- Colour scale limits (explicit cutoffs) --- ScatterPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", color_by = "w",             lower_cutoff = 0, upper_cutoff = 1)

title: Atomic scatter plot (internal) — ScatterPlotAtomic
text: Core implementation drawing single scatter plot. workhorse behind exported ScatterPlot function – takes single data frame (split_by support) returns ggplot object. plot positions points x y coordinates (numeric), optional size encoding via third numeric variable (size_by), colour encoding (color_by) can numeric (continuous gradient) categorical (discrete palette). Key features: Variable point size – size_by accepts either numeric constant (uniform dot size) column name (size scales value via scale_size_area()). Colour modes – color_by can numeric (rendered continuous gradient via scale_fill_gradientn() scale_color_gradientn()) factor/character (discrete palette via scale_fill_manual()/scale_color_manual()). Shape support – shapes 21–25 support separate fill border (color) aesthetics; shapes use colour aesthetic. border_color parameter can constant colour, TRUE (track fill gradient), omitted. Colour scale trimming – lower_quantile / upper_quantile (explicit lower_cutoff / upper_cutoff) trim/clamp continuous colour scale extremes. Axis transformation – xtrans ytrans apply arbitrary scale transformations (e.g. "log10", "sqrt") x y axes via scale_x_continuous() / scale_y_continuous(). Point highlighting – highlight accepts indices, rownames, logical TRUE, string expression select points overlaid distinct shape, size, colour, alpha.

title: Atomic scatter plot (internal) — ScatterPlotAtomic
text: ggplot dispatch – selects gglogger::ggplot ggplot2::ggplot based getOption("plotthis.gglogger.enabled"). Column resolution – facet_by validated forced factor via check_columns(). size_by validated column name; numeric constants kept -. color_by validated. Dummy colour guard – color_by = NULL, synthetic .color_by column (constant "") created colour legend suppressed. Categorical colour coercion – color_by non-numeric column, forced factor via check_columns() force_factor = TRUE. Continuous colour scale preparation – color_by numeric, prepare_continuous_color_scale() computes quantile-trimmed cutoff-clamped colour range endpoints (feat_colors_value) winsorizes --range data. Highlight resolution – highlight processed subset data frame (hidata) via one four paths: TRUE (points), numeric index vector, single string expression (parsed rlang::parse_expr() applied via filter()), character vector rownames. error thrown rownames used data without row names. Shape fill detection – determines whether point shape (shape) supports separate fill aesthetic (shapes 21–25). Base ggplot – initialises ggplot(data, aes(x, y)). Point layer configuration – aesthetic mapping constant aesthetics geom_point() assembled: Fill vs colour: shapes 21–25 map fill (optionally color border_color = TRUE); shapes map color. Size: numeric size_by passed constant aesthetic; column name mapped size aesthetic. Size scale – size_by column name, scale_size_area(max_size = 6) added size legend (order = 1, title = size_name %||% size_by). Fill / colour scale (4 branches): Shape fill + numeric colour: scale_fill_gradientn() feat_colors_value rescaling either colour-bar legend suppressed guide. border_color = TRUE, matching scale_color_gradientn() added. Shape fill + factor colour: scale_fill_manual() palette_this() colours discrete legend. border_color = TRUE, matching scale_color_manual() added. Shape without fill + numeric colour: scale_color_gradientn() continuous colour-bar legend. Shape without fill + factor colour: scale_color_manual() discrete legend. Highlight overlay – hidata non-NULL, second geom_point() layer added using highlight_shape, highlight_color, highlight_size, highlight_alpha. fill colour aesthetic chosen based whether highlight_shape 21–25. Axis scales – scale_x_continuous(trans = xtrans) scale_y_continuous(trans = ytrans) apply requested transformations. Labels theme – labs(title, subtitle, x, y) sets plot annotations. do_call(theme, theme_args) applies theme. panel.grid.major set grey80 dashed lines. Dimension calculation – calculate_plot_dimensions() computes plot height width base_height = 5, aspect.ratio, legend metrics (number legend items maximum label character width). Faceting – facet_plot() wraps plot facet_wrap / facet_grid facet_by provided.

title: Atomic split bar plot (internal) — SplitBarPlotAtomic
text: Core implementation drawing split (divergent / waterfall-style) bar plot.  Bars extend left (negative values) right (positive values) central zero line, y-axis listing categories.  Bars can coloured categorical continuous fill variable, opacity can encode additional numeric variable via alpha_by. workhorse behind exported SplitBarPlot (also aliased WaterfallPlot).

text: data data frame. x character string specifying numeric column x-axis. title: Atomic split bar plot (internal) — SplitBarPlotAtomic
text: Column resolution — x, y, fill_by, alpha_by, facet_by validated via check_columns.  Multi-column y fill_by concatenated respective separators. Direction assignment — direction_name column created sign x, assigning row positive negative group (customisable via direction_pos_name / direction_neg_name). Fill resolution — fill_by NULL, direction column used fill (two-colour palette). Categorical fills use discrete colour scale; numeric fills use prepare_continuous_color_scale() quantile / cutoff clamping scale_fill_gradientn(). Alpha — alpha_by provided, bar opacity encodes additional numeric variable via scale_alpha_continuous(). Ordering — order_y named list controlling vertical ordering bars.  keys "+" "-" specify separate orderings positive negative bars; key "*" applies single ordering bars.  Values character vectors ordering criteria: "x_asc", "x_desc", "alpha_asc", "alpha_desc". Empty level padding — keep_empty_y = TRUE, missing y-levels padded per facet (globally) zero-height bars still appear axis. Text labels — category names drawn beside bars via geom_text() zero line.  flipped, labels rotated 90°.  Long labels wrapped via str_wrap() using max_charwidth. Plot assembly — ggplot built geom_vline(xintercept = 0) centre line, geom_col() bars, appropriate fill / alpha scales. Dimension calculation — calculate_plot_dimensions() computes dimensions number y categories, scaled bar_height / 4.  Flipping adjusts aspect ratio. Faceting — facet_plot() wraps result, defaulting facet_scales = "free_y".

code: # \donttest{ data <- data.frame(     x = factor(rep(c("A", "B", NA, "D"), 3), levels = LETTERS[1:4]),     y = c(1, 3, 6, 5, 4, 2, 5, 7, 8, 9, 4, 8),     group = factor(rep(c("F1", NA, "F3"), each = 4), levels = c("F1", "F2", "F3")) )  # Basic trend plot with grouping TrendPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", group_by = "group")   # Scaled to proportions TrendPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", group_by = "group",          scale_y = TRUE)   # Split into sub-plots (no group_by -- single-colour fill) TrendPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", split_by = "group")   # Per-split palettes TrendPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", split_by = "group",           palette = c(F1 = "Set1", F3 = "Dark2"))   # How keep_na and keep_empty work TrendPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", group_by = "group",          keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = TRUE)  TrendPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", group_by = "group",          keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = list(x = FALSE, group = 'level'))   # Faceting TrendPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", facet_by = "group",          keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = list(x = FALSE, group = 'level'))  # }

title: Atomic trend plot (internal) — TrendPlotAtomic
text: Column resolution – x, y, group_by, facet_by validated transformed via check_columns. Multi-column inputs x group_by concatenated single columns using respective separators (x_sep, group_by_sep). Count aggregation – y = NULL, count observations unique (x, group_by, facet_by) combination computed new .count column. Factor levels preserved aggregation. Proportion scaling – scale_y = TRUE, y-values divided sum within (x, facet_by) group, producing proportion (0–1). Percent labels used automatically y-axis. NA / empty-level handling – process_keep_na_empty() applies keep_na keep_empty policies. Per-column keep_empty settings extracted x, group_by, facet_by independently. facet columns must identical keep_empty values. Note keep_empty = TRUE supported globally empty categories break continuity x-axis. Group fill setup – group_by = NULL, dummy .fill factor created single group still draws first palette colour. legend suppressed (legend.position = "none"). Data completion – complete() pads x group_by (facet_by) combinations y = 0. prevents geom_area() interpolating across missing groups, otherwise cause stacked areas exceed correct total. Area layer construction – completed data expanded duplicating row offsetting x-axis positions \(\pm 0.2\) numeric scale, creating discrete-width area strips align perfectly bars. Colour mapping – palette_this() assigns colours group_by levels, including NA (defaulting "grey80"). Plot assembly – ggplot object built geom_area() (half alpha, grey outline) background fill, overlaid geom_col() (full alpha, black outline, width 0.4) foreground bars. layers use position_stack(vjust = 0.5). x-axis uses scale_x_discrete() y-axis uses scale_y_continuous() (percent labels scaled). fill scale drop argument controlled keep_empty_group. Dimension calculation – calculate_plot_dimensions() computes plot height width x-axis category count, aspect.ratio, legend metrics. base height increases plots 10 x categories (4.5 6.5 inches). resulting height width attributes stored ggplot object. Faceting – facet_plot() wraps plot facet_wrap / facet_grid facet_by provided, respecting keep_empty setting facet variables.

title: UpSet Plot — UpsetPlot
text: Draws UpSet plot visualising set intersections set sizes.  plot comprises: horizontal bar chart showing size intersection, filled intersection count. combination matrix (rows = sets, columns = intersections) membership dots connecting lines. set-size bar chart left matrix (added automatically ggupset). function accepts data four formats: List — named list element vectors (one per set). Long — data frame one row per (set, element) pair. Wide — data frame row element set logical 0/1 membership column. UpsetPlotData — pre-processed object prepare_upset_data(). Supports splitting sub-plots via split_by, per-split colour palettes legend control, combining sub-plots via patchwork.

text: ggplot dispatch — selects gglogger::ggplot ggplot2::ggplot based getOption("plotthis.gglogger.enabled"). Data preparation — prepare_upset_data() converts input internal UpsetPlotData format Intersection list-column (set-membership labels per element) "group_order" attribute set ordering. Text scale — base_size theme_args (default 12) drives text_size_scale proportionally scales combination-matrix row gap, label sizes, point sizes. Bar geom — geom_bar() counts rows per intersection. Bars filled count via scale_fill_gradientn() colours palette_this(). Bar labels — label = TRUE, geom_text_repel() places count labels bar, configurable foreground, background, size. Scale x upset — ggupset::scale_x_upset(...) called, returning two components: ScaleUpset (index [[1]]) CoordCombMatrix (index [[2]]).  scale applied; bundled coord discarded avoid "Coordinate system already present" conflict. Labels theme — labs() sets title, subtitle, axis text.  do_call(theme, theme_args) applies chosen theme.  Aspect ratio, legend position, legend direction, grid lines configured. Combination matrix — ggupset::axis_combmatrix() builds lower panel via override_plotting_function: Reorders rows match group_order (reversed top--bottom set listing). Alternating row backgrounds via geom_rect() white / #F7F7F7 fills. Set-membership dots (geom_point()) black present #E0E0E0 absent. Connecting lines (geom_line()) observed dots trace intersection pattern. Matrix theme — ggupset::theme_combmatrix() applies combination-matrix appearance combmatrix_gap rows (scaled text_size_scale). Dimension calculation — calculate_plot_dimensions() computes height n_sets width n_intersections, passed via ... (default 99).  aspect.ratio used standard H/W coupling x_scale_factor = 0.6.  additional width adjustment accounts set-label character width.

title: Cell velocity plot — VelocityPlot
text: Plots RNA velocity vectors low-dimensional embedding (e.g., UMAP, t-SNE) visualize direction magnitude cellular state transitions. Supports three visualization modes: raw arrows cell position, arrows regular grid, streamline paths. Optionally colors arrows cell metadata groups.

title: Cell velocity plot — VelocityPlot
code: VelocityPlot(   embedding,   v_embedding,   plot_type = c("raw", "grid", "stream"),   split_by = NULL,   group_by = NULL,   group_name = "Group",   group_palette = "Paired",   group_palcolor = NULL,   n_neighbors = NULL,   density = 1,   smooth = 0.5,   scale = 1,   min_mass = 1,   cutoff_perc = 5,   arrow_angle = 20,   arrow_color = "black",   arrow_alpha = 1,   keep_na = FALSE,   keep_empty = FALSE,   streamline_l = 5,   streamline_minl = 1,   streamline_res = 1,   streamline_n = 15,   streamline_width = c(0, 0.8),   streamline_alpha = 1,   streamline_color = NULL,   streamline_palette = "RdYlBu",   streamline_palcolor = NULL,   palreverse = FALSE,   streamline_bg_color = "white",   streamline_bg_stroke = 0.5,   aspect.ratio = 1,   title = "Cell velocity",   subtitle = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   legend.position = "right",   legend.direction = "vertical",   theme = "theme_this",   theme_args = list(),   return_layer = FALSE,   seed = 8525 )

title: Cell velocity plot — VelocityPlot
text: embedding matrix data frame dimension n_obs x n_dim specifying low-dimensional embedding coordinates (e.g., UMAP, t-SNE) cells. first two columns used x y axes. v_embedding matrix data frame dimension n_obs x n_dim specifying velocity vectors cell. Must dimensions embedding. plot_type character string specifying visualization method. "raw" plots arrows directly cell's embedding position. "grid" averages velocities onto regular grid plots arrows grid points. "stream" computes smooth streamline paths gridded velocity field. Default "raw". split_by supported VelocityPlot. Setting parameter raise error. group_by optional vector length number rows embedding specifying grouping variable cells. provided, arrows colored group using group_palette. applies plot_type = "raw"; ignored warning "grid" "stream". Default NULL. group_name character string specifying legend title grouping variable. Default "Group". group_palette character string specifying color palette use grouping variable. Passed palette_this. Default "Paired". group_palcolor optional character vector specific colors grouping variable. NULL, colors generated group_palette. Default NULL. n_neighbors integer value specifying number nearest neighbors computing grid velocities. used plot_type "grid" "stream". Default ceiling(nrow(embedding) / 50). density numeric value specifying grid density along dimension. used plot_type "grid" "stream". plot_type = "raw", density 0 1, specifies fraction cells randomly subsample. Default 1. smooth numeric value specifying standard deviation multiplier Gaussian kernel averaging cell velocities onto grid points. used plot_type "grid" "stream". Default 0.5. scale numeric value specifying scaling factor velocity vectors. Applied raw grid arrows. plot_type = "stream", fixed 1 internally. Default 1. min_mass numeric value specifying minimum mass threshold retaining grid points. used plot_type "grid" "stream". Default 1. cutoff_perc numeric value specifying percentile cutoff removing low-density grid points. used plot_type "stream". Default 5. arrow_angle numeric value specifying angle arrowheads degrees. Applied arrow plot_type "raw" "grid". Default 20. arrow_color character string specifying color velocity arrows. plot_type = "stream", sets arrowhead color. Default "black". arrow_alpha numeric value 0 1 specifying transparency velocity arrows. used plot_type = "raw" "grid"; plot_type = "stream", use streamline_alpha instead. Default 1. keep_na logical character value specifying handle NA values group_by. Unlike plot functions, VelocityPlot support named lists per-column control. See keep_na common_args details supported values. Default FALSE. keep_empty One FALSE, TRUE, "level" specifying handle empty factor levels group_by. Unlike plot functions, VelocityPlot support named lists per-column control. See keep_empty common_args details. Default FALSE. streamline_l numeric value specifying integration length streamlines. Passed geom_streamline L parameter. Default 5. streamline_minl numeric value specifying minimum streamline length. Shorter streamlines drawn. Passed geom_streamline min.L parameter. Default 1. streamline_res numeric value specifying resolution streamline integration. Passed geom_streamline res parameter. Default 1. streamline_n numeric value specifying number streamlines draw. Passed geom_streamline n parameter. Default 15. streamline_width numeric vector length 2 specifying range line widths streamlines. Passed scale_size(range = ...). used streamline_color NULL. Default c(0, 0.8). streamline_alpha numeric value 0 1 specifying transparency velocity streamlines. Default 1. streamline_color optional character string specifying fixed color streamlines. NULL (default), streamlines colored velocity magnitude using streamline_palette. streamline_palette character string specifying color palette streamline velocity magnitude. Passed palette_this. used streamline_color NULL. Default "RdYlBu". streamline_palcolor optional character vector specific colors streamline velocity gradient. NULL, colors generated streamline_palette. Default NULL. palreverse logical value indicating whether reverse palette. Default FALSE. streamline_bg_color character string specifying background (outline) color applied streamlines create stroke effect. Default "white". streamline_bg_stroke numeric value specifying additional line width background stroke relative foreground streamline. Default 0.5. aspect.ratio numeric value specifying aspect ratio plot. title character string specifying title plot. function can used generate title based default title. useful split_by used title needs dynamic. subtitle character string specifying subtitle plot. xlab character string specifying x-axis label. ylab character string specifying y-axis label. legend.position character string specifying position legend. waiver(), single groups, legend "none", otherwise "right". legend.direction character string specifying direction legend. theme character string theme class (.e. ggplot2::theme_classic) specifying theme use. Default "theme_this". theme_args list arguments pass theme function. return_layer logical value indicating whether return ggplot layers instead full assembled plot. TRUE, returns list ggplot layers suitable combining ggplot objects. Default FALSE. seed random seed use. Default 8525.

title: Cell velocity plot — VelocityPlot
text: ggplot object representing cell velocity plot, height width attributes set consistent rendering. return_layer = TRUE, returns list ggplot layers instead.

title: Cell velocity plot — VelocityPlot
text: VelocityPlot function proceeds following steps: Input validation — Verifies embedding v_embedding matrices data frames equal dimensions, group_by matches number rows (provided), split_by NULL (unsupported raises error). Axis label resolution — Uses column names embedding axis labels, falling back "Reduction 1" "Reduction 2" column names NULL. Grouping setup — Converts group_by factor applies keep_na / keep_empty logic filter recode missing values empty factor levels. Plot-type dispatch — Branches plot_type: raw — Optionally subsamples cells density < 1, scales velocity vectors scale, computes arrow lengths proportional embedding range, renders geom_segment arrowheads. group_by provided, arrows colored group using group_palette. grid — Delegates .compute_velocity_on_grid interpolate sparse cell velocities onto regular grid, renders geom_segment arrowheads grid point. group_by ignored warning. stream — Delegates .compute_velocity_on_grid adjust_for_stream = TRUE, renders smooth streamline paths via geom_streamline. streamline_color provided, streamlines use fixed color background stroke; NULL, streamlines colored velocity magnitude using streamline_palette. group_by ignored warning. Layer return plot assembly — return_layer = TRUE, returns list ggplot layers. Otherwise, constructs full ggplot object labels, theme, aspect ratio, legend configuration, height / width attributes via calculate_plot_dimensions().

title: Cell velocity plot — VelocityPlot
code: # \donttest{ data(dim_example) dim_example$clusters[dim_example$clusters == "Ductal"] <- NA  # Basic velocity plot with group coloring VelocityPlot(dim_example[, 1:2], dim_example[, 3:4], group_by = dim_example$clusters)   # Handle NA groups with keep_na / keep_empty VelocityPlot(dim_example[, 1:2], dim_example[, 3:4], group_by = dim_example$clusters,     keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = TRUE)  VelocityPlot(dim_example[, 1:2], dim_example[, 3:4], group_by = dim_example$clusters,     keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = 'level')  VelocityPlot(dim_example[, 1:2], dim_example[, 3:4], group_by = dim_example$clusters,     keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = FALSE)  # }

title: Venn / Euler diagram — VennDiagram
text: Draws Venn Euler diagrams visualise overlap relationships among multiple sets.  Supports four input formats: long (one row per element-set pair), wide (logical/0-1 columns per set), named list (element vectors per set), pre-computed VennPlotData object. Intersection regions can filled continuous colour gradient encoding element count (fill_mode = "count" / "count_rev") blended set colours (fill_mode = "set").  Region labels can display counts, percentages, , custom function.  Set labels always show set name total element count. Use split_by produce separate Venn diagrams level grouping variable.  Note split_by supported data data frame (list VennPlotData inputs split).

title: Venn / Euler diagram — VennDiagram
code: VennDiagram(   data,   in_form = c("auto", "long", "wide", "list", "venn"),   split_by = NULL,   split_by_sep = "_",   group_by = NULL,   group_by_sep = "_",   id_by = NULL,   label = "count",   label_fg = "black",   label_size = NULL,   label_bg = "white",   label_bg_r = 0.1,   fill_mode = "count",   palreverse = FALSE,   fill_name = NULL,   palette = ifelse(fill_mode == "set", "Paired", "Blues"),   palcolor = NULL,   alpha = 1,   theme = "theme_this",   theme_args = list(),   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   legend.position = "right",   legend.direction = "vertical",   aspect.ratio = 1,   combine = TRUE,   nrow = NULL,   ncol = NULL,   byrow = TRUE,   seed = 8525,   axes = NULL,   axis_titles = axes,   guides = NULL,   design = NULL,   ... )

text: non-NULL split_by provided input data frame: Validation — error raised data data frame (list VennPlotData input split). Column resolution — split_by validated optionally concatenated via check_columns() force_factor = TRUE allow_multi = TRUE. title: Venn / Euler diagram — VennDiagram
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(8525) data <- list(     A = sort(sample(letters, 8)),     B = sort(sample(letters, 8)),     C = sort(sample(letters, 8)),     D = sort(sample(letters, 8)) )  # Basic Venn diagram with count labels VennDiagram(data)   # Fill by set membership (blended colours) VennDiagram(data, fill_mode = "set")   # Show both count and percentage VennDiagram(data, label = "both")   # Custom label function using set names VennDiagram(data, label = function(df) df$name)   # Custom palette and transparency VennDiagram(data, palette = "material-indigo", alpha = 0.6)  # }

title: Atomic Venn / Euler diagram (internal) — VennDiagramAtomic
text: Core implementation drawing Venn Euler diagram visualises overlap relationships among multiple sets.  Supports four input formats (long, wide, list, pre-computed VennPlotData) normalised prepare_venn_data() internal VennPlotData representation used ggVennDiagram. Intersection regions can filled either continuous colour gradient based element count (fill_mode = "count" "count_rev") blended set colours (fill_mode = "set").  Region labels can display raw count, percentage total elements, , nothing, custom function result.  Set labels always show set name total element count.

title: Atomic Venn / Euler diagram (internal) — VennDiagramAtomic
text: Data type detection — detect_venn_datatype() identifies whether data long, wide, list, VennPlotData format. Data preparation — prepare_venn_data() converts input VennPlotData object suitable rendering ggVennDiagram. Geometry extraction — ggVennDiagram utilities (venn_regionedge(), venn_setedge(), venn_regionlabel(), venn_setlabel()) compute polygon vertices label positions sets intersections. Fill resolution — fill_mode = "set", palette_this() assigns colours set; blend_colors() helper blends colours overlapping sets intersection region.  fill_mode = "count" "count_rev", continuous gradient applied across regions via scale_fill_gradientn() colour bar legend. Label computation — Region labels formatted per label parameter: raw count, percentage, , none, custom function receives data frame columns "id", "X", "Y", "name", "item", "count".  Set labels formatted "setName\n(count)". Plot assembly — geom_polygon() draws filled regions; geom_path() draws set outlines; two geom_text_repel() layers add region labels set labels.  fill_mode = "set", colours mapped directly (aesthetic mapping, legend); count modes, scale_fill_gradientn() continuous scale used. Theme coordinate system — coord_equal() enforces square aspect ratio.  theme removes axis text, ticks, titles, grid lines, panel borders.  x-axis expansion widened accommodate set label text width. Dimension calculation — calculate_plot_dimensions() computes height width attributes (inches) base_height, aspect.ratio, legend metrics.  fill_mode = "set", legend position forced "none".

title: Volcano plot — VolcanoPlot
text: Produces volcano plot — scatter plot displays statistical significance (typically -log10 adjusted p-value) y-axis versus magnitude change (log2 fold change) x-axis. Points coloured automatically significance category ("sig_pos_x", "sig_neg_x", "insig") user-supplied column. significant features can labelled automatically via geom_text_repel(), specific points can highlighted. function supports automatic labelling top features (distance origin), mirrored layout via flip_negatives, x-axis trimming reduce influence extreme values, faceting, splitting separate sub-plots via split_by per-split colour palette legend control.

title: Volcano plot — VolcanoPlot
code: VolcanoPlot(   data,   x,   y,   ytrans = function(n) -log10(n),   color_by = NULL,   color_name = NULL,   xlim = NULL,   flip_negatives = FALSE,   x_cutoff = NULL,   y_cutoff = 0.05,   split_by = NULL,   split_by_sep = "_",   label_by = NULL,   x_cutoff_name = NULL,   y_cutoff_name = NULL,   x_cutoff_color = "red2",   y_cutoff_color = "blue2",   x_cutoff_linetype = "dashed",   y_cutoff_linetype = "dashed",   x_cutoff_linewidth = 0.5,   y_cutoff_linewidth = 0.5,   pt_size = 2,   pt_alpha = 0.5,   nlabel = 5,   labels = NULL,   label_size = 3,   label_fg = "black",   label_bg = "white",   label_bg_r = 0.1,   highlight = NULL,   highlight_color = "red",   highlight_size = 2,   highlight_alpha = 1,   highlight_stroke = 0.5,   trim = c(0, 1),   facet_by = NULL,   facet_scales = "fixed",   facet_ncol = NULL,   facet_nrow = NULL,   facet_byrow = TRUE,   theme = "theme_this",   theme_args = list(),   palette = "Spectral",   palcolor = NULL,   palreverse = FALSE,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   aspect.ratio = 1,   legend.position = "right",   legend.direction = "vertical",   seed = 8525,   combine = TRUE,   nrow = NULL,   ncol = NULL,   byrow = TRUE,   axes = NULL,   axis_titles = axes,   guides = NULL,   design = NULL,   ... )

title: Atomic volcano plot (internal) — VolcanoPlotAtomic
text: Core implementation drawing single volcano plot. workhorse behind exported VolcanoPlot function — takes single data frame (split_by support) returns ggplot object. plot displays statistical significance (typically -log10 adjusted p-value) y-axis versus magnitude change (log2 fold change) x-axis, points coloured significance category user-supplied variable. Top features can automatically labelled via geom_text_repel(), specific points can highlighted. function categorises points three groups based cutoff thresholds: "sig_pos_x" (points exceeding positive x-cutoff y-cutoff), "sig_neg_x" (points exceeding negative x-cutoff y-cutoff), "insig" (remaining points). color_by = NULL, categorisation drives point colouring; otherwise supplied column controls colour scale.
