title: Atomic volcano plot (internal) — VolcanoPlotAtomic
text: ggplot dispatch — selects gglogger::ggplot ggplot2::ggplot based getOption("plotthis.gglogger.enabled"). Input validation — trim (must length-2 [0, 1]) xlim (must length-2 NULL) validated via stopifnot(). trim sorted. Column resolution — x, y, color_by, facet_by, label_by validated transformed via check_columns. Multi-column facet_by concatenated force_factor = TRUE. y-axis transformation — y-column transformed ytrans() (default: -log10(n)). y_cutoff value also transformed. x_cutoff defaulting — x_cutoff NULL, set 0 (suppressing x-cutoff legend line). Category assignment — .category factor levels c("sig_neg_x", "insig", "sig_pos_x") created: y_cutoff non-NULL: points |x| > x_cutoff y > y_cutoff significant. y_cutoff NULL: x-cutoff determines significance. Color resolution — three cases: color_by = NULL: uses .category discrete colour column; legend suppressed. Character/factor column: discrete colour scale via palette_this() scale_color_manual(), guide suppressed. Numeric column: continuous gradient via scale_color_gradientn() framed colour-bar legend. Flip negatives — flip_negatives = TRUE, y-values points negative x multiplied -1, creating mirrored volcano - -regulated features show significance side y-axis. Label column — .label populated label_by rownames(data). Trim / winsorize — x-values beyond trim quantile bounds clamped. bounds nonzero opposite sign, symmetrised smaller absolute value. Outlying points marked .outlier. Label selection — two modes: Explicit labels: specified rows (name index) marked labelling. Automatic: top nlabel points (Euclidean distance origin) selected per sign(x) group, per facet level facet_by set. labels filtered exclude "insig" points. Data split — data split pos_data (x >= 0) neg_data (x < 0) ggrepel labels can nudge opposite directions (positive points nudge left, negative points nudge right). Outlier jitter — outlier points rendered separately position_jitter() reduce overplotting. Base ggplot — geom_point() layers positive, negative, outlier data, colour mapped color_by. Colour scale — discrete: scale_color_manual() "insig" forced "grey" (palcolor NULL); continuous: scale_color_gradientn() palette re-scaled colour-bar centred 0. Highlight — highlight provided, two additional geom_point() layers (non-outliers outliers jitter) overlay highlighted points highlight_color. x-cutoff lines — vertical dashed lines +/- x_cutoff via geom_vline() new_scale_color(), labelled x_cutoff_name. Suppressed x_cutoff NULL 0. y-cutoff lines — horizontal dashed line(s) y_cutoff (+/- y_cutoff flip_negatives = TRUE) via geom_hline() new_scale_color(), labelled y_cutoff_name. Flip-negatives axis — flip_negatives = TRUE, solid geom_hline(yintercept = 0) added scale_y_continuous(labels = abs) formats y-axis. x-axis limits — optional xlim passed ggplot2::xlim(). Reference line labels — grey80 dashed vertical line x = 0, followed geom_text_repel() positive negative labelled points separate x-nudges. Labels theme — labs(), coord_cartesian(clip = ""), do_call(theme, theme_args), theme elements aspect.ratio, legend.position, legend.direction. Dimension calculation — calculate_plot_dimensions() computes height width attributes base_height = 5, aspect.ratio, legend geometry. Faceting — facet_plot() wraps plot facet_wrap / facet_grid facet_by provided.

title: Word cloud plot — WordCloudPlot
text: Draws word cloud plot visualises word frequency importance. Words displayed font size proportional count variable colour based continuous score variable, using geom_text_wordcloud rendering. function supports pre-tokenised words (via word_by) sentence splitting (via sentence_by). Sentences automatically lowercased, stripped punctuation, split individual words aggregation. Common stop words can excluded via words_excluded, number displayed words controlled top_words. word cloud can faceted (via facet_by) split separate sub-plots via split_by. split_by used, split level receives word cloud, results combined single layout via combine_plots.

title: Word cloud plot — WordCloudPlot
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(8525) data <- data.frame(     word = c("apple", "banana", "cherry", "date", "elderberry",              "fig", "grape", "honeydew", "kiwi", "lemon"),     count = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 15, 25, 35, 45, 55),     score = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 1.5, 2.5, 3.5, 4.5, 5.5),     facet = rep(c("Group1", "Group2"), each = 5),     split = rep(c("A", "B"), 5) )  # Basic word cloud with word, count, and score columns WordCloudPlot(data, word_by = "word",               count_by = "count", score_by = "score")   # Word cloud using sentence_by (sentences split into words) data_sent <- data.frame(     sentence = c("The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog",                  "A quick brown dog jumps over a lazy fox"),     score = c(10, 5) ) WordCloudPlot(data_sent, sentence_by = "sentence", score_by = "score")   # Word cloud with faceting WordCloudPlot(data, word_by = "word",               count_by = "count", score_by = "score",               facet_by = "facet")   # Word cloud split by a grouping variable WordCloudPlot(data, word_by = "word",               count_by = "count", score_by = "score",               split_by = "split")  # }

title: Word cloud plot (internal) — WordCloudPlotAtomic
text: Core implementation drawing word cloud plot. workhorse behind exported WordCloudPlot function — takes single data frame (split_by support) returns ggplot object rendered via geom_text_wordcloud. function supports two input modes: Pre-tokenized words (word_by) — row contains single word (multiple words list column unnested). Sentence splitting (sentence_by) — row contains sentence phrase; function splits text individual words removing punctuation lowercasing. Words displayed font size proportional count variable (count_by) colour based continuous score variable (score_by). Text filtering options allow removal short words, common stop words, bracketed patterns.

title: Word cloud plot (internal) — WordCloudPlotAtomic
text: ggplot dispatch — selects gglogger::ggplot ggplot2::ggplot based getOption("plotthis.gglogger.enabled"). Input validation — ensures exactly one word_by sentence_by specified. Errors provided neither provided. sentence_by used, count_by must NULL (counts derived occurrences splitting). Column resolution — facet_by, count_by, score_by validated transformed via check_columns. Default values — score_by NULL, synthetic .score column value 1 created. count_by NULL, synthetic .count column value 1 created. Sentence splitting branch (sentence_by set): sentence_by column validated via check_columns. Text lowercased, punctuation removed, string split individual words whitespace boundaries. Words unnested separate rows via unnest. Data grouped word (facet_by columns present) aggregated: sum(count_by) score_agg(score_by). Separate aggregation templates handle 0, 1, 2 facet_by columns. Word branch (word_by set): word_by column validated via check_columns. Column values unnested separate rows (supports list columns row may contain multiple words). Data grouped word (facet_by columns present) aggregated similarly sentence branch. Text filtering pipeline — aggregated data processed sequentially: Words matching pattern [.*] (bracketed content) removed. Words fewer minchar characters removed. Words listed words_excluded removed (case-insensitive matching). Duplicate rows removed. top top_words words score retained via slice_max. Angle assignment — word randomly assigned rotation angle 0 (60\ probability), creating characteristic word cloud appearance. Colour scale preparation — palette_this() type = "continuous" generates smooth gradient score variable. colors_value sequence created spanning min(score) quantile(score, 0.99) colour interpolation. Plot assembly — ggplot object built : ggwordcloud::geom_text_wordcloud() renders words rm_outside = TRUE, square shape, eccentricity 1. scale_color_gradientn() maps score continuous colour gradient, colour-bar legend (titled score_name "Score"). scale_size() maps count font size within word_size range, size legend (titled count_name "Count"). coord_flip() swaps axes (word cloud convention). do_call(theme, theme_args) applies selected theme; aspect.ratio, legend.position, legend.direction set directly. Dimension calculation — calculate_plot_dimensions() computes plot height width using base_height = 4.5, aspect.ratio, legend metrics. resulting height / width attributes stored ggplot object. Faceting — facet_plot() wraps plot facet_wrap facet_grid facet_by provided, maximum 2 facet columns.

title: Bar plot — BarPlot
text: Draws bar plots flexible fill, grouping, labelling, annotation options.  Supports simple single-colour bars grouped bars (dodged stacked).  Bars can filled categorical variable (discrete colour scale), continuous variable (colour gradient), fixed colour. function supports count aggregation (omit y plot observation counts), proportion scaling (scale_y = TRUE grouped bars), background stripes (add_bg), bar labels, trend lines, horizontal reference lines, splitting separate sub-plots via split_by. SplitBarPlot (also known WaterfallPlot) draws divergent bar plot bars extend left (negative values) right (positive values) central zero line.  bar fill colour opacity can encode additional variables, vertical ordering categories fully customisable. function supports split_by produce separate panels, facet_by grouped views within panels, alpha_by encoding secondary numeric variable via opacity.

title: Bar plot — BarPlot
text: data data frame. x character string specifying column name data frame plot x-axis. x_sep character string join multiple x columns. Default "_". y character string specifying column name data frame plot y-axis. flip Logical; TRUE, swap x y axes. fill_by variable used fill bars.  categorical numeric columns accepted: TRUE (default) — fill x-axis values. FALSE — solid fill (first palette colour). column name (character/factor) — discrete colour scale. column name (numeric) — continuous gradient quantile / cutoff controls. Ignored group_by provided (fill determined group_by). fill_name character string fill legend title.  applies group_by = NULL fill fill_by. line_name Legend name reference line. label_nudge numeric value controlling distance labels bar top, expressed fraction data range. label column name (TRUE) text labels bars. TRUE, y-axis values labelled.  column name, values column used. label_fg character string specifying label text colour. label_size numeric value specifying label text size. label_bg character string specifying label background colour. label_bg_r numeric value specifying label background corner radius. group_by Columns group data plotting plotting functions support multiple groups, concatenated one column, using group_by_sep separator group_by_sep separator multiple group_by columns. See group_by group_name character string group fill legend title. NULL, group_by column name used. split_by column(s) split data separate sub-plots. split_by_sep Separator concatenated split_by columns. facet_by character string specifying column name data frame facet plot. Otherwise, data split split_by generate multiple plots combine one using patchwork::wrap_plots facet_scales Whether scale axes facets. Default "fixed" options "free", "free_x", "free_y". See ggplot2::facet_wrap facet_ncol numeric value specifying number columns facet. facet_by single column facet_wrap used. facet_nrow numeric value specifying number rows facet. facet_by single column facet_wrap used. facet_byrow logical value indicating whether fill plots row. Default TRUE. facet_args list additional arguments passed faceting function (e.g., scales, labeller). add_bg Logical; add alternating background stripes behind bars. bg_palette Palette background stripes. bg_palcolor Custom colours background stripes. bg_alpha Alpha transparency background stripes. add_line numeric y-intercept horizontal reference line. line_color Colour reference line. line_width Width reference line. line_type Linetype reference line (e.g., 1 = solid, 2 = dashed). add_trend Logical; add trend line points connecting bar tops. trend_color Colour trend line. trend_linewidth Width trend line. trend_ptsize Size trend line points. theme character string theme class (.e. ggplot2::theme_classic) specifying theme use. Default "theme_this". theme_args list arguments pass theme function. palette character string specifying palette use. named list vector can used specify palettes different split_by values. palcolor character string specifying color use palette. named list can used specify colors different split_by values. values missing, values palette used (palcolor NULL values). palreverse logical value indicating whether reverse palette. Default FALSE. alpha numeric value specifying transparency plot. lower_quantile, upper_quantile Lower upper quantiles continuous color/fill scale. actual cutoffs determined quantiles lower_cutoff upper_cutoff NULL. Defaults: lower_quantile = 0, upper_quantile = 0.99. lower_cutoff, upper_cutoff Explicit lower upper cutoffs continuous color/fill scale. NULL (default), cutoffs determined lower_quantile upper_quantile via quantile. Values outside [lower_cutoff, upper_cutoff] range clamped (winsorized) nearest cutoff value. x_text_angle numeric value specifying angle x-axis text. aspect.ratio numeric value specifying aspect ratio plot. y_min, y_max Numeric limits y-axis (x-axis flipped). position character string specifying bar layout: "auto" (default: dodge ≤5 groups, stack otherwise), "dodge" (side--side), "stack" (stacked top ). position_dodge_preserve character string passed position_dodge2(): "total" preserves overall bar group width; "single" preserves individual bar widths. legend.position character string specifying position legend. waiver(), single groups, legend "none", otherwise "right". legend.direction character string specifying direction legend. title character string specifying title plot. function can used generate title based default title. useful split_by used title needs dynamic. subtitle character string specifying subtitle plot. xlab character string specifying x-axis label. ylab character string specifying y-axis label. keep_empty One FALSE, TRUE "level". can also take named list specify different behavior different columns. Without named list, behavior applies categorical/character columns used plot, example, x, group_by, fill_by, etc. FALSE (default): Drop empty factor levels data plotting. TRUE: Keep empty factor levels show separate category plot. "level": Keep empty factor levels, show plot. assigned colors palette maintain consistency across multiple plots. Alias: levels keep_na logical value character replace NA values data. can also take named list specify different behavior different columns. TRUE NA, NA values replaced NA. FALSE, NA values removed data plotting. character string provided, NA values replaced provided string. named vector/list provided, names column names apply behavior , values one TRUE, FALSE, character string. Without named vector/list, behavior applies categorical/character columns used plot, example, x, group_by, fill_by, etc. expand values expand x y axes. like CSS padding. single value provided, used axes sides. two values provided, first value used top/bottom side second value used left/right side. three values provided, first value used top side, second value used left/right side, third value used bottom side. four values provided, values used top, right, bottom, left sides, respectively. can also use named vector specify values side. axis discrete, values applied 'add' 'expansion' function. axis continuous, values applied 'mult' 'expansion' function. See also https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/expansion.html width numeric value specifying bar width (0–1). combine Logical; TRUE (default), returns combined patchwork object.  FALSE, returns named list ggplot objects. ncol, nrow Integer number columns / rows combined layout. byrow Logical; fill combined layout row (default TRUE). seed numeric seed reproducibility. axes, axis_titles Character strings axis handling combined layout. guides Character string legend collection across panels. design custom layout design combined plot. ... Additional arguments. y_sep character string join multiple y columns. Default "_". alpha_by character string naming numeric column encode bar opacity.  Default NULL (bars fully opaque). alpha_reverse Logical; TRUE, reverse alpha scale direction (solid low values, transparent high). alpha_name character string alpha legend title. order_y named list controlling vertical ordering bars. Keys "+" (positive bars), "-" (negative bars), "*" (bars).  Values character vectors ordering criteria: "x_asc", "x_desc", "alpha_asc", "alpha_desc".  Default orders positive bars descending x descending alpha; negative bars descending x ascending alpha. bar_height numeric value (0–1) specifying bar height fraction available category slot. lineheight numeric value controlling line height wrapped category labels. max_charwidth integer specifying maximum character width wrapping category labels. fill_by_sep character string join multiple fill_by columns.  Default "_". direction_name character string naming internal direction column (used legends).  Default "direction". direction_pos_name character string labelling positive direction legend.  Default "positive". direction_neg_name character string labelling negative direction legend.  Default "negative". x_min, x_max Numeric limits x-axis.  NULL, symmetric limits computed maximum absolute x-value.

title: Bar plot — BarPlot
code: # \donttest{ data <- data.frame(     x = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F", "G", "H"),     y = c(10, 8, 16, 4, 6, 12, 14, 2),     group = c("G1", "G1", "G2", "G2", "G3", "G3", "G4", "G4"),     facet = c("F1", "F2", "F3", "F4", "F1", "F2", "F3", "F4") )  # Single-colour bars BarPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y")   # Solid fill (no colour mapping) BarPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", fill_by = FALSE)   # Label bar tops BarPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", label = TRUE)  BarPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", label = "facet", label_nudge = 0)   # Grouped bars BarPlot(data, x = "group", y = "y", group_by = "x")   # Dodged bars with background stripes BarPlot(data,     x = "group", y = "y", group_by = "x",     position = "dodge", add_bg = TRUE)   # split_by with faceting BarPlot(data,     x = "x", y = "y", split_by = "group",     facet_by = "facet", position = "dodge", facet_ncol = 1)   # split_by with collected guides BarPlot(data,     x = "x", y = "y", split_by = "group", facet_by = "facet",     position = "dodge", facet_ncol = 1, guides = 'collect')   # Per-split palettes BarPlot(data,     x = "x", y = "y", split_by = "group",     palette = list(G1 = "Reds", G2 = "Blues", G3 = "Greens", G4 = "Purp"),     facet_by = "facet", position = "dodge", facet_ncol = 1)   # Background stripe palette BarPlot(data,     x = "group", y = "y", group_by = "x",     position = "dodge", add_bg = TRUE, bg_palette = "Spectral")   # Count bars (y = NULL) BarPlot(data, x = "group", ylab = "count")   # Flipped axes BarPlot(data, x = "group", flip = TRUE, ylab = "count")   # Numeric fill_by with colour gradient BarPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", fill_by = "y", flip = TRUE)   # Control fill colour scale limits (quantile) BarPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", fill_by = "y", flip = TRUE,         lower_quantile = 0.1, upper_quantile = 0.9)   # Control fill colour scale limits (explicit cutoff) BarPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", fill_by = "y", flip = TRUE,         lower_cutoff = 5, upper_cutoff = 12)   # keep_na and keep_empty examples data <- data.frame(     x = factor(c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F", NA, "H"),                levels = LETTERS[1:10]),     y = c(10, 8, 16, 4, 6, NA, 14, 2),     group = factor(c("G1", "G1", "G2", NA, "G3", "G3", "G5", "G5"),                    levels = c("G1", "G2", "G3", "G4", "G5")),     facet = factor(c("F1", NA, "F3", "F4", "F1", "F2", "F3", "F4"),                    levels = c("F1", "F2", "F3", "F4", "F5")) )  # Default: NA and empty levels dropped BarPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y") #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_col()`).   # Keep both NA and empty levels BarPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y",         keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = TRUE) #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_col()`).   # With faceting BarPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y",         keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = TRUE, facet_by = "facet") #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_col()`).   # Keep NA, hide empty levels but reserve their colours BarPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y",         keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = 'level') #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_col()`).   # Per-column keep_na / keep_empty BarPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y",         keep_na = list(x = TRUE), keep_empty = list(x = FALSE)) #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_col()`).   # Grouped bars with keep_na / keep_empty BarPlot(data, x = "group", y = "y", group_by = "x") #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_col()`).  BarPlot(data, x = "group", y = "y", group_by = "x",         keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = TRUE) #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_col()`).  BarPlot(data, x = "group", y = "y", group_by = "x",         keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = TRUE, facet_by = "facet") #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_col()`).   # Per-column on grouped bars BarPlot(data, x = "group", y = "y", group_by = "x",         keep_na = list(x = TRUE, group = FALSE),         keep_empty = list(x = FALSE, group = TRUE)) #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_col()`).  # } # \donttest{ set.seed(8525) data <- data.frame(     word = c("apple", "banana", "cherry", "date", "elderberry",              "It is a very long term with a lot of words"),     count = c(-10, 20, -30, 40, 50, 34),     score = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 3.2),     group = c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C", "C") )  # Basic split bar plot with alpha encoding SplitBarPlot(data, x = "count", y = "word", alpha_by = "score")   # Control label wrapping SplitBarPlot(data, x = "count", y = "word", alpha_by = "score",              max_charwidth = 30, lineheight = 1.1)   # Fill by categorical variable SplitBarPlot(data, x = "count", y = "word", fill_by = "group")   # Faceting SplitBarPlot(data, x = "count", y = "word", facet_by = "group",              fill_name = "Direction")   # Per-split palettes SplitBarPlot(data, x = "count", y = "word", alpha_by = "score",              split_by = "group",              palette = c(A = "Reds", B = "Blues", C = "Greens"))   # keep_na and keep_empty examples data <- data.frame(     word = factor(c("apple", "banana", "cherry", NA, "elderberry",          "It is a very long term with a lot of words"),          levels = c("apple", "banana", "cherry", "date", "elderberry",              "unused", "It is a very long term with a lot of words")),     count = c(-10, 20, NA, 40, 10, 34),     score = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 3.2),     group = factor(sample(c("A", "A", "B", "B", "C", "C")),          levels = c("A", "B", "C", "D")) )  # Default: NA and empty levels dropped SplitBarPlot(data, x = "count", y = "word", alpha_by = "score")   # Keep NA and empty levels SplitBarPlot(data, x = "count", y = "word", alpha_by = "score",              keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = TRUE)   # Keep with faceting SplitBarPlot(data, x = "count", y = "word", alpha_by = "score",              keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = TRUE, facet_by = "group")   # Keep NA, hide empty levels (reserve colours) SplitBarPlot(data, x = "count", y = "word", alpha_by = "score",              keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = "level")   # Per-column control SplitBarPlot(data, x = "count", y = "word", alpha_by = "score",              keep_na = list(word = FALSE), keep_empty = list(word = TRUE))   # Control fill colour scale limits SplitBarPlot(data, x = "count", y = "word", fill_by = "score",              lower_cutoff = 1, upper_cutoff = 4)  # }

title: Box / Bar plot — BoxPlot
text: Draws box plots bar plots (mean ± error bars) extensive customisation options.  Supports jittered beeswarm points, paired observations connecting lines, trend lines, statistical test annotations (pairwise omnibus), background stripes, reference lines, point highlighting, custom summary statistic overlays. public API — delegates BoxViolinPlot base = "box" base = "bar", turn dispatches BoxViolinPlotAtomic split_by level. Draws violin plots extensive customisation options.  Supports jittered beeswarm points, box plot overlays, trend lines, statistical test annotations, background stripes, reference lines, point highlighting, custom summary statistic overlays. public API — delegates BoxViolinPlot base = "violin", dispatches BoxViolinPlotAtomic split_by level. Draws beeswarm plots — points arranged beeswarm algorithm avoid overlap displaying distribution density.  convenience wrapper delegates BoxViolinPlot base = "none" add_beeswarm = TRUE. Requires ggbeeswarm package.  get beeswarm plot box plot, use BeeswarmPlot(..., add_box = TRUE).  get violin plot beeswarm points, use ViolinPlot(..., add_beeswarm = TRUE).

title: Box / Bar plot — BoxPlot
text: data data frame. x character string specifying column name data frame plot x-axis. x_sep character string join multiple x columns. Default "_". y character string specifying column name data frame plot y-axis. base character string: "box" (default) "bar". Bar plots show group means optional error bars. in_form character string: "long" (default) "wide".  wide form, x columns pivoted long format. split_by column(s) split data separate sub-plots. split_by_sep Separator concatenated split_by columns. symnum_args list arguments passed symnum symbolic p-value coding. sort_x R expression string (e.g., "mean(y)") order x-axis categories.  Default NULL keeps original order. keep_empty_x TRUE, empty levels placed last. flip Logical; TRUE, swap x y axes. keep_empty One FALSE, TRUE "level". can also take named list specify different behavior different columns. Without named list, behavior applies categorical/character columns used plot, example, x, group_by, fill_by, etc. FALSE (default): Drop empty factor levels data plotting. TRUE: Keep empty factor levels show separate category plot. "level": Keep empty factor levels, show plot. assigned colors palette maintain consistency across multiple plots. Alias: levels keep_na logical value character replace NA values data. can also take named list specify different behavior different columns. TRUE NA, NA values replaced NA. FALSE, NA values removed data plotting. character string provided, NA values replaced provided string. named vector/list provided, names column names apply behavior , values one TRUE, FALSE, character string. Without named vector/list, behavior applies categorical/character columns used plot, example, x, group_by, fill_by, etc. group_by Columns group data plotting plotting functions support multiple groups, concatenated one column, using group_by_sep separator group_by_sep separator multiple group_by columns. See group_by group_name character string dodge legend title. paired_by character string naming column identifies paired observations.  Forces add_point = TRUE connects paired observations lines. x_text_angle numeric value specifying angle x-axis text. step_increase Fractional step increase stacking significance brackets multiple comparisons exist. fill_mode character string controlling fill colour mapping: "dodge" (fill group_by, discrete), "x" (fill x-axis categories, discrete), "mean" "median" (fill pre-computed statistic, continuous gradient). palreverse logical value indicating whether reverse palette. Default FALSE. position_dodge_preserve Passed position_dodge(): "total" preserves overall group width; "single" preserves individual element width. theme character string theme class (.e. ggplot2::theme_classic) specifying theme use. Default "theme_this". theme_args list arguments pass theme function. palette character string specifying palette use. named list vector can used specify palettes different split_by values. palcolor character string specifying color use palette. named list can used specify colors different split_by values. values missing, values palette used (palcolor NULL values). alpha numeric value specifying transparency plot. aspect.ratio numeric value specifying aspect ratio plot. legend.position character string specifying position legend. waiver(), single groups, legend "none", otherwise "right". legend.direction character string specifying direction legend. add_point Logical; add jittered beeswarm points plot. pt_color Colour points.  add_beeswarm = TRUE pt_color NULL, points coloured fill variable. pt_size Numeric size points.  Default computed data size: min(3000 / nrow(data), 0.6). pt_alpha Numeric transparency points. jitter_width Numeric width jitter.  Defaults 0.5, set 0 paired_by provided. jitter_height Numeric height jitter.  Default 0. stack Logical; stack facetted panels compact layout shared strip labels. y_max, y_min Numeric y-axis limits, quantile notation strings (e.g., "q95" 95th percentile, "q5" 5th percentile). add_beeswarm Logical; use ggbeeswarm::geom_beeswarm() non-overlapping point layout instead jitter.  Requires ggbeeswarm package. beeswarm_method Beeswarm layout method: "swarm", "compactswarm", "hex", "square", "center". beeswarm_cex Numeric scaling point spacing.  Larger values spread points . beeswarm_priority Point layout priority: "ascending", "descending", "density", "random". beeswarm_dodge Numeric dodge width beeswarm points group_by provided.  Default 0.9. add_trend Logical; add trend lines connecting group medians. trend_color Colour trend line.  NULL group_by present, lines coloured per group. trend_linewidth Width trend line. trend_ptsize Size trend line points. add_stat summary function (e.g., mean, median) display point shape legend entry. stat_name Legend title stat summary shape. stat_color Colour stat summary point. stat_size Size stat summary point. stat_stroke Stroke width stat summary point. stat_shape Shape (integer) stat summary point.  Uses scale_shape_identity() shape rendered directly. add_errorbar Type error bars bar plots.  See Details. errorbar_color, errorbar_width, errorbar_linewidth Error bar appearance controls. add_bg Logical; add alternating background stripes. bg_palette Palette background stripes. bg_palcolor Custom colours background stripes. bg_alpha Alpha transparency background stripes. add_line numeric y-intercept horizontal reference line. line_color Colour reference line. line_width Width reference line. line_type Linetype reference line. highlight specification points highlight: TRUE (), numeric index vector, logical expression string, character vector row names. highlight_color Colour highlighted points. highlight_size Size highlighted points. highlight_alpha Alpha highlighted points. comparisons logical value (TRUE pairs) list two-element vectors specifying pairwise comparisons.  available fill_mode = "dodge" (.e., group_by present). ref_group character string specifying reference group comparisons. pairwise_method Method pairwise tests.  Default "wilcox.test". multiplegroup_comparisons Logical; perform omnibus test (e.g., Kruskal-Wallis) across groups. multiple_method Method omnibus test.  Default "kruskal.test". sig_label Label format significance annotations.  pairwise comparisons: "p.format", "p.signif", glue template (e.g., "p = {p}").  multiple-group tests: "p.format" "p.signif". sig_labelsize Size significance label text. hide_ns Logical; hide non-significant comparison labels. facet_by character string specifying column name data frame facet plot. Otherwise, data split split_by generate multiple plots combine one using patchwork::wrap_plots facet_scales Whether scale axes facets. Default "fixed" options "free", "free_x", "free_y". See ggplot2::facet_wrap facet_ncol numeric value specifying number columns facet. facet_by single column facet_wrap used. facet_nrow numeric value specifying number rows facet. facet_by single column facet_wrap used. facet_byrow logical value indicating whether fill plots row. Default TRUE. title character string specifying title plot. function can used generate title based default title. useful split_by used title needs dynamic. subtitle character string specifying subtitle plot. xlab character string specifying x-axis label. ylab character string specifying y-axis label. seed numeric seed reproducibility. combine Logical; TRUE (default), returns combined patchwork object.  FALSE, returns named list ggplot objects. ncol, nrow Integer number columns / rows combined layout. byrow Logical; fill combined layout row (default TRUE). axes, axis_titles Character strings axis handling combined layout. guides Character string legend collection across panels. ... Additional arguments. add_box Logical; overlay box plot primary geometry. Mutually exclusive base = "box" base = "bar". box_color Colour overlaid box plot outline fill. box_width Width overlaid box plot. box_ptsize Size median point overlaid box plot. add_violin Logical; whether add violin plot behind beeswarm points.  supported — function stop error directing use ViolinPlot(..., add_beeswarm = TRUE) instead.

title: Box / Bar plot — BoxPlot
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(8525) data <- data.frame(     x = rep(LETTERS[1:8], each = 40),     y = c(rnorm(160), rnorm(160, mean = 1)),     group1 = sample(c("g1", "g2"), 320, replace = TRUE),     group2 = sample(c("h1", "h2", "h3", "h4"), 320, replace = TRUE) )  # Basic box plot BoxPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y")   # With beeswarm points BoxPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", add_beeswarm = TRUE, pt_color = "grey30")   # Stacked + flipped + faceted BoxPlot(data,     x = "x", y = "y",     stack = TRUE, flip = TRUE, facet_by = "group1",     add_bg = TRUE, bg_palette = "Paired")   # Stacked + flipped + split_by with per-split colours BoxPlot(data,     x = "x", y = "y",     stack = TRUE, flip = TRUE, split_by = "group1",     add_bg = TRUE, bg_palette = "Paired",     palcolor = list(g1 = c("red", "blue"), g2 = c("blue", "red")))   # sort_x — order by mean(y) data <- data.frame(   x = factor(rep(LETTERS[1:5], each = 40),      levels = c(LETTERS[1:2], "unused", LETTERS[3:5])),   y = c(rnorm(40, mean = 5), rnorm(40, mean = 4), rnorm(40, mean = 3),      rnorm(40, mean = 2), rnorm(40, mean = 1)) ) BoxPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", sort_x = "mean(y)", keep_empty = TRUE)  BoxPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", sort_x = "mean(-y)", keep_empty = TRUE)   # Wide-form data data_wide <- data.frame(A = rnorm(100), B = rnorm(100), C = rnorm(100)) BoxPlot(data_wide, x = c("A", "B", "C"), in_form = "wide")   # Paired observations with connecting lines and paired test paired_data <- data.frame(     subject = rep(paste0("s", 1:10), each = 2),     visit = rep(c("pre", "post"), times = 10),     value = rnorm(20)) BoxPlot(paired_data,     x = "visit", y = "value", comparisons = TRUE,     paired_by = "subject", add_point = TRUE)   # Paired + grouped paired_group_data <- data.frame(     subject = rep(paste0("s", 1:6), each = 2),     x = rep(c("A", "B"), each = 6),     group = rep(c("before", "after"), times = 6),     value = rnorm(12)) BoxPlot(paired_group_data,     x = "x", y = "value",     paired_by = "subject", group_by = "group",     comparisons = TRUE, pt_size = 3, pt_color = "red") #> Warning: Forcing 'add_point' = TRUE when 'paired_by' is provided. #> Warning: [Box/Violin/BeeswarmPlot] Some pairwise comparisons may fail due to insufficient data points or variability. Adjusting data to ensure valid comparisons.   # keep_na and keep_empty examples data <- data.frame(     x = factor(rep(c(LETTERS[1:3], NA, LETTERS[5:8]), each = 40),        levels = c(LETTERS[1:8])),     y = c(rnorm(160), rnorm(160, mean = 1)),     group1 = sample(c("g1", "g2"), 320, replace = TRUE),     group2 = factor(sample(c("h1", NA, "h3", "h4"), 320, replace = TRUE),        levels = c("h1", "h2", "h3", "h4")))  BoxPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y")  BoxPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = TRUE)  BoxPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = TRUE,         facet_by = "group2")  BoxPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = 'level')  BoxPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", group_by = "group2")  BoxPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", group_by = "group2",         keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = TRUE)  BoxPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", group_by = "group2",         keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = 'level')   # Per-column keep_na / keep_empty BoxPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", group_by = "group2",         keep_na = list(x = TRUE, group2 = FALSE),         keep_empty = list(x = FALSE, group2 = TRUE))   # Bar plot (base = "bar") data$y <- abs(data$y) BoxPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", base = "bar")  BoxPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", base = "bar", add_errorbar = "SD")  BoxPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", base = "bar", add_errorbar = "CI95")  BoxPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", base = "bar", add_errorbar = "none")  BoxPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", base = "bar", group_by = "group1")  BoxPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", base = "bar", add_point = TRUE)  BoxPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", base = "bar",         fill_mode = "mean", palette = "Blues")  # } # \donttest{ ViolinPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y")  ViolinPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", add_beeswarm = TRUE, pt_color = "grey30")  ViolinPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", add_box = TRUE)  ViolinPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", add_point = TRUE)  ViolinPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", add_trend = TRUE)  ViolinPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", add_stat = mean)  ViolinPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", add_bg = TRUE)  ViolinPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", add_line = 0)   # Grouped ViolinPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", group_by = "group1")   # Grouped + faceted + box overlay ViolinPlot(data,     x = "x", y = "y", group_by = "group1",     facet_by = "group2", add_box = TRUE) #> Warning: Groups with fewer than two datapoints have been dropped. #> ℹ Set `drop = FALSE` to consider such groups for position adjustment purposes. #> Warning: Groups with fewer than two datapoints have been dropped. #> ℹ Set `drop = FALSE` to consider such groups for position adjustment purposes.   # Highlight ViolinPlot(data,     x = "x", y = "y", add_point = TRUE,     highlight = 'group1 == "g1"', alpha = 0.8,     highlight_size = 1.5, pt_size = 1, add_box = TRUE)   # Pairwise comparisons with formatted labels ViolinPlot(data,     x = "x", y = "y", group_by = "group1",     comparisons = TRUE, sig_label = "p = {p}")   # Explicit comparison list + hide non-significant ViolinPlot(data,     x = "x", y = "y", sig_label = "p.format", hide_ns = TRUE,     facet_by = "group2", comparisons = list(c("D", "E"))) #> Warning: Computation failed in `stat_pwc()`. #> Caused by error in `map()`: #> ℹ In index: 1. #> Caused by error in `wilcox.test.default()`: #> ! not enough 'y' observations #> Warning: Computation failed in `stat_pwc()`. #> Caused by error in `map()`: #> ℹ In index: 1. #> Caused by error in `wilcox.test.default()`: #> ! not enough 'y' observations #> Warning: Computation failed in `stat_pwc()`. #> Caused by error in `map()`: #> ℹ In index: 1. #> Caused by error in `wilcox.test.default()`: #> ! not enough 'y' observations #> Warning: Computation failed in `stat_pwc()`. #> Caused by error in `map()`: #> ℹ In index: 1. #> Caused by error in `wilcox.test.default()`: #> ! not enough 'y' observations #> Warning: Computation failed in `stat_pwc()`. #> Caused by error in `map()`: #> ℹ In index: 1. #> Caused by error in `wilcox.test.default()`: #> ! not enough 'y' observations #> Warning: Computation failed in `stat_pwc()`. #> Caused by error in `map()`: #> ℹ In index: 1. #> Caused by error in `wilcox.test.default()`: #> ! not enough 'y' observations #> Warning: Computation failed in `stat_pwc()`. #> Caused by error in `map()`: #> ℹ In index: 1. #> Caused by error in `wilcox.test.default()`: #> ! not enough 'y' observations #> Warning: Computation failed in `stat_pwc()`. #> Caused by error in `map()`: #> ℹ In index: 1. #> Caused by error in `wilcox.test.default()`: #> ! not enough 'y' observations #> Warning: Computation failed in `stat_pwc()`. #> Caused by error in `map()`: #> ℹ In index: 1. #> Caused by error in `wilcox.test.default()`: #> ! not enough 'y' observations   # Continuous fill (mean) + omnibus test ViolinPlot(data,     x = "x", y = "y", fill_mode = "mean",     facet_by = "group2", palette = "Blues",     multiplegroup_comparisons = TRUE) #> Warning: The following aesthetics were dropped during statistical transformation: fill. #> ℹ This can happen when ggplot fails to infer the correct grouping structure in #>   the data. #> ℹ Did you forget to specify a `group` aesthetic or to convert a numerical #>   variable into a factor? #> Warning: The following aesthetics were dropped during statistical transformation: fill. #> ℹ This can happen when ggplot fails to infer the correct grouping structure in #>   the data. #> ℹ Did you forget to specify a `group` aesthetic or to convert a numerical #>   variable into a factor? #> Warning: The following aesthetics were dropped during statistical transformation: fill. #> ℹ This can happen when ggplot fails to infer the correct grouping structure in #>   the data. #> ℹ Did you forget to specify a `group` aesthetic or to convert a numerical #>   variable into a factor?   # Per-split palettes ViolinPlot(data,     x = "x", y = "y", fill_mode = "mean",     split_by = "group1", palette = c(g1 = "Blues", g2 = "Reds"))   # Stacked faceting ViolinPlot(data,     x = "x", y = "y", stack = TRUE,     facet_by = "group2", add_box = TRUE, add_bg = TRUE,     bg_palette = "Paired")  # } # \donttest{ # Basic beeswarm BeeswarmPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y")   # Control point size BeeswarmPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", pt_size = 1)   # Beeswarm with box overlay BeeswarmPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", add_box = TRUE, pt_color = "grey30")   # Grouped BeeswarmPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", group_by = "group1")   # Grouped without dodging BeeswarmPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", group_by = "group1",              beeswarm_dodge = NULL)   # Hex layout with wider spacing BeeswarmPlot(data,     x = "x", y = "y", beeswarm_method = "hex",     beeswarm_cex = 2) #> Warning: In `position_beeswarm`, method `hex` discretizes the data axis (a.k.a the #> continuous or non-grouped axis). #> This may result in changes to the position of the points along that axis, #> proportional to the value of `cex`. #> To prevent this behavior, set `preserve.data.axis=TRUE`. #> This warning is displayed once per session.  # title: Chord / Circos plot — ChordPlot
text: Draws chord diagram (also known circos plot) visualise relationships two categorical variables.  Categories arranged around circle, connecting ribbons (links) represent flow association source target nodes.  width link proportional associated numeric value observation count. function supports count aggregation (omit y plot observation counts per pair), link colouring source target node, label rotation options, splitting separate sub-diagrams via split_by. CircosPlot alias ChordPlot. CircosPlot alias ChordPlot.

code: # \donttest{ set.seed(8525) data <- data.frame(     nodes1 = sample(c("Soure1", "Source2", "Source3"), 10, replace = TRUE),     nodes2 = sample(letters[1:3], 10, replace = TRUE),     y = sample(1:5, 10, replace = TRUE) )  # Basic chord diagram (counts) ChordPlot(data, from = "nodes1", to = "nodes2")   # Links coloured by target + rotated labels ChordPlot(data, from = "nodes1", to = "nodes2",           links_color = "to", labels_rot = TRUE)   # With explicit y values (link thickness) ChordPlot(data, from = "nodes1", to = "nodes2", y = "y")   # Split by a column — one diagram per split level ChordPlot(data, from = "nodes1", to = "nodes2", split_by = "y")   # Per-split palettes ChordPlot(data, from = "nodes1", to = "nodes2", split_by = "y",           palette = c("1" = "Reds", "2" = "Blues",                       "3" = "Greens", "4" = "Purp"))   # Flip source/target direction ChordPlot(data, from = "nodes1", to = "nodes2", flip = TRUE)  # }

title: Density Plot / Histogram — DensityPlot
text: Density plot visualising distribution numeric variable. Uses ggplot2::geom_density() render smooth kernel density estimates, optional grouping, faceting, split-splitting, data-distribution rug bars along baseline. public entry point density plots; companion Histogram() function provides binned-histogram rendering. dispatch internal engine (DensityHistoPlotAtomic) type = "density" type = "histogram" respectively. Histogram visualising distribution numeric variable via binned counts. Uses ggplot2::geom_histogram(), optional trend-line overlays, zero-skip interpolation, grouping, faceting, split-splitting. histogram companion DensityPlot(). dispatch internal engine (DensityHistoPlotAtomic) type = "histogram" type = "density" respectively. use_trend = TRUE, histogram bars replaced entirely point--line trend; add_trend = TRUE, trend overlaid top bars. trend_skip_zero option uses zoo::na.approx() interpolate across empty bins continuous trend curve — particularly useful transformed y-axes.

title: Density Plot / Histogram — DensityPlot
text: split_by specified, DensityPlot() executes following pipeline: Argument validation — validate_common_args() checks seed facet-consistency. NA / empty normalisation — check_keep_na() / check_keep_empty() convert keep_na / keep_empty per-column lists. Theme resolution — process_theme() resolves theme string theme function. Split column resolution — check_columns() validates split_by (force_factor, concat_multi). Pre-filtering — process_keep_na_empty() removes NA / empty levels split column, data split split_by levels (order preserved). Per-split parameter resolution — check_palette(), check_palcolor(), check_legend() resolve palette, palcolor, legend.position, legend.direction split. Per-split dispatch — split passed DensityHistoPlotAtomic(type = "density", ...) resolved parameters. Title defaults split level name unless title function. Combination — combine_plots() assembles list plots via patchwork::wrap_plots(), applying nrow, ncol, byrow, axes, axis_titles, guides, design. split_by specified, Histogram() executes following pipeline: Argument validation — validate_common_args() checks seed facet-consistency. NA / empty normalisation — check_keep_na() / check_keep_empty() convert keep_na / keep_empty per-column lists. Theme resolution — process_theme() resolves theme string theme function. Split column resolution — check_columns() validates split_by (force_factor, concat_multi). Pre-filtering — process_keep_na_empty() removes NA / empty levels split column, data split split_by levels (order preserved). Per-split parameter resolution — check_palette(), check_palcolor(), check_legend() resolve palette, palcolor, legend.position, legend.direction split. Per-split dispatch — split passed DensityHistoPlotAtomic(type = "histogram", ...) resolved parameters (including bins, binwidth, use_trend, add_trend, trend_skip_zero, trend_alpha, trend_linewidth, trend_pt_size). Title defaults split level name unless title function. Combination — combine_plots() assembles list plots via patchwork::wrap_plots(), applying nrow, ncol, byrow, axes, axis_titles, guides, design.

title: Density Plot / Histogram — DensityPlot
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(8525) data <- data.frame(     x = c(rnorm(500, -1), rnorm(500, 1)),     group = factor(rep(c("A", NA, "C", "D"), each = 250), levels = LETTERS[1:4]),     facet = sample(c("F1", "F2"), 1000, replace = TRUE) )  # basic density DensityPlot(data, x = "x")  DensityPlot(data, x = "x", group_by = "group")   # NA / empty level handling DensityPlot(data, x = "x", group_by = "group",     keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = TRUE)  DensityPlot(data, x = "x", group_by = "group",     keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = 'level')   # faceting and splitting DensityPlot(data, x = "x", group_by = "group", facet_by = "facet")  DensityPlot(data, x = "x", split_by = "facet", add_bars = TRUE)  DensityPlot(data, x = "x", split_by = "facet", add_bars = TRUE,     palette = c(F1 = "Set1", F2 = "Set2"))  # } set.seed(8525) data <- data.frame(     x = sample(setdiff(1:100, c(30:36, 50:55, 70:77)), 1000, replace = TRUE),     group = factor(rep(c("A", "B", NA, "D"), each = 250), levels = LETTERS[1:4]),     facet = sample(c("F1", "F2"), 1000, replace = TRUE) )  # basic histogram Histogram(data, x = "x") #> Using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`.  Histogram(data, x = "x", group_by = "group") #> Using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`.   # NA / empty level handling Histogram(data, x = "x", group_by = "group", keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = 'level') #> Using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`.   # add_bars and trend overlays Histogram(data, x = "x", split_by = "facet", add_bars = TRUE) #> Using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`. #> Using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`.  Histogram(data, x = "x", group_by = "group", add_trend = TRUE) #> Using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`.  Histogram(data, x = "x", group_by = "group", add_trend = TRUE, trend_skip_zero = TRUE) #> Using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`.   # use_trend replaces bars entirely Histogram(data, x = "x", group_by = "group", split_by = "facet",  use_trend = TRUE, trend_pt_size = 3) #> Using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`. #> Using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`.   # per-split palettes Histogram(data, x = "x", group_by = "group", split_by = "facet",  palette = c(F1 = "Paired", F2 = "Spectral")) #> Using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`. #> Using `bins = 30`. Pick better value with `binwidth`.

title: DimPlot / FeatureDimPlot — DimPlot
text: Visualise dimension reduction data (PCA, t-SNE, UMAP, etc.) 2D 3D scatter plot. DimPlot() colours points discrete grouping variable (e.g., clusters), FeatureDimPlot() colours points continuous numeric feature (e.g., gene expression, lineage scores). functions share internal engine (DimPlotAtomic) support extensive set annotation layers: group boundary marks, network/graph edges, 2D density contours, lineage/trajectory curves, RNA-velocity arrows (raw, grid, stream), statistical summary mini-plots group centroids, point highlighting, background context points facets, flexible label positioning. dims 3 elements, functions automatically return interactive plotly 3D scatter plot (via DimPlotAtomic3D). Certain 2D-features silently ignored 3D mode (see @param dims full list). Rendering scales dataset size: standard geom_point() small data, automatic rasterisation via scattermore::geom_scattermore() nrow(data) > 1e5, hex-bin aggregation (geom_hex() / stat_summary_hex()). Feature expression dimension reduction plots. Colours points continuous numeric variable (e.g., gene expression, module score, lineage pseudotime) using gradient colour scale, optional quantile trimming background cutoff. multiple features provided facet_by set, data automatically pivoted long format faceted feature name. split_by = TRUE dispatches feature separate plot independent layout control. split_by column name splits column's levels, producing one plot per level per-split palette support. detailed split_by workflows, see main DimPlot / FeatureDimPlot documentation (@section split_by Workflow (FeatureDimPlot)).

title: DimPlot / FeatureDimPlot — DimPlot
text: data data frame. dims character vector column names plot x, y (optionally z) axes numeric vector column indices. 3 dimensions provided, 3D interactive plot created using plotly. Supported 3D: group_by, features, labels, highlight, lineages, graph/network, show_stat, order. supported 3D: add_mark, stat_by, add_density, velocity, hex, facet_by, raster. group_by Columns group data plotting plotting functions support multiple groups, concatenated one column, using group_by_sep separator group_by_sep separator multiple group_by columns. See group_by split_by character vector column names split data plot separately. TRUE, data split features — feature plotted panel. Use instead facet_by need independent layout control (nrow, ncol) per-feature palettes via split_by = TRUE combined palette / palcolor. split_by_sep separator multiple split_by columns. See split_by pt_size numeric value point size. NULL (default), point size auto-calculated min(3000 / nrow(data), 0.6) large datasets automatically get smaller points. pt_alpha numeric value [0, 1] point transparency. Default 1. bg_color character string specifying colour used NA-valued points background context points drawn facets. Default "grey80". label_insitu logical value. TRUE, raw group names placed group median coordinates instead numeric indices. Forces label = TRUE. Default FALSE. show_stat logical value. TRUE (default), number points per group shown legend labels subtitle. Ignored theme = "theme_blank". label logical value. TRUE, group labels (numeric indices default, group names label_insitu = TRUE) placed median coordinates group. Forced TRUE label_repel label_insitu set. label_size numeric value label text size. Passed ggrepel::geom_text_repel(). Default 4. label_fg character string label text (foreground) colour. Default "white". label_bg character string label background / outline colour. Default "black". label_bg_r numeric value background fill ratio label bounding box. Passed ggrepel::geom_text_repel(bg.r = ...). Default 0.1. label_repel logical value. TRUE, labels repelled force label_repulsion. visible point anchor drawn. Forces label = TRUE. label_repulsion numeric value repulsion force label_repel = TRUE. Passed ggrepel::geom_text_repel(force = ...). Default 20. label_pt_size numeric value size anchor point drawn label_repel = TRUE. Default 1. label_pt_color character string colour label anchor point. Default "black". label_segment_color character string colour line segment connecting label anchor. Used non-repel mode (label_repel = FALSE) min.segment.length = 0. Default "black". order character string controlling draw order points: "-" (default) — row order data preserved. "reverse" — rows reversed. "high-top" — points high values (last factor levels group_by) drawn last (top). "low-top" — points low values (first factor levels) drawn last. "random" — rows randomly shuffled. high-top low-top, NA values always plotted bottom. applied group_by, draw order changes — legend colours order unaffected. Within level, point order preserved. precise control, set factor levels plotting. See https://github.com/pwwang/scplotter/issues/29#issuecomment-3009694130 examples. highlight specification highlighted points: NULL (default): highlighting. TRUE: highlight points (adds dark outline around every point). character string: dplyr filter expression (e.g., "clusters == 'Ductal'"). character vector: row names highlight. numeric vector: row indices highlight. highlight_alpha numeric value [0, 1] transparency highlighted points. Default 1. highlight_size numeric value size inner (coloured) highlight point. Default 1. highlight_color character string colour outer highlight ring. Default "black". highlight_stroke numeric value thickness outer highlight ring (difference outer ring size highlight_size). Default 0.8. add_mark logical value. TRUE, group boundaries drawn around points using ggforce marks. Requires group_by. supported 2D. mark_type character string specifying mark shape. Options: "hull" (convex hull, default), "ellipse", "rect", "circle". mark_expand unit value outward expansion mark boundary. Passed ggforce::geom_mark_*(expand = ...). Default unit(3, "mm"). mark_alpha numeric value [0, 1] transparency mark fill. Default 0.1. mark_linetype numeric value line type mark boundary. Default 1 (solid). stat_by character string naming column used compute per-group statistical summary mini-plots embedded group centroid positions. supported group_by (features). supported 2D without facet_by. stat_plot_type character string specifying mini-plot type. Options: "pie" (default), "ring", "bar", "line". stat_plot_size numeric value size stat mini-plot, expressed fraction axis range. Default 0.1. stat_args list additional arguments passed stat plot function (e.g., list(palette = "Set1")). Default list(palette = "Set1"). graph specification network / graph edges overlay. Sources: character string starting "@" (e.g., "@graph"): extracts attribute named "graph" attributes(data). Graph object (e.g., Seurat): coerced dense matrix via .matrix(). matrix, data.frame, dgCMatrix: used directly adjacency matrix. Numeric indices character column names: extracts columns data. Edges drawn non-zero, lower-triangle entries. Requires data row names matching matrix dimnames. edge_size numeric vector length 2 specifying range [min, max] scale_linewidth_continuous(range = ...) applied edge widths. Default c(0.05, 0.5). edge_alpha numeric value [0, 1] transparency graph edges. Default 0.1. edge_color character string colour graph edges. Default "grey40". add_density logical value. TRUE, 2D density layer overlaid. supported 2D. density_color character string colour density contour lines. Used density_filled = FALSE. Default "grey80". density_filled logical value. TRUE, density rendered filled raster (stat_density_2d(geom = "raster")) instead contour lines. separate fill scale used. density_filled_palette character string naming palette filled density layer. Default "Greys". density_filled_palcolor character vector specific colours filled density palette. Default NULL (auto-resolved density_filled_palette). lineages character vector column names representing pseudotime / trajectory lineages. column fitted LOESS smooth (span = lineages_span, degree = 2) across 2D embedding, trimming top bottom lineages_trim quantiles. supported 2D without facet_by. lineages_trim numeric vector length 2 specifying lower upper quantile thresholds [0, 1] trimming lineage values LOESS fitting. Default c(0.01, 0.99). lineages_span numeric value passed span stats::loess() controlling smoothness lineage curve. Smaller values follow data closely. Default 0.75. lineages_palette character string naming palette lineage colours. Default "Dark2". lineages_palcolor character vector specific colours lineage curves. Default NULL (auto-resolved lineages_palette). lineages_arrow ggplot2 arrow specification applied end lineage paths. Default arrow(length = unit(0.1, "inches")). lineages_linewidth numeric value width lineage curve lines. Default 1. lineages_line_bg character string colour background (wider) stroke drawn behind lineage curve improved visibility. Default "white". lineages_line_bg_stroke numeric value additional width background stroke relative lineages_linewidth. background line total width lineages_linewidth + lineages_line_bg_stroke. Default 0.5. lineages_whiskers logical value. TRUE, short line segments connect smoothed lineage curve original data coordinates fitted points. Default FALSE. lineages_whiskers_linewidth numeric value width whisker lines. Default 0.5. lineages_whiskers_alpha numeric value [0, 1] transparency whisker lines. Default 0.5. velocity specification RNA-velocity arrows. Can : NULL (default): velocity overlay. character / integer vector: column names indices data velocity embedding. data frame matrix: velocity embedding (must align data rows). supported 2D without facet_by. velocity_plot_type character string specifying velocity rendering style. Options: "raw" (arrows embedding), "grid" (grid-based arrows), "stream" (streamlines). Default "raw". velocity_n_neighbors numeric value number neighbours used velocity grid computation. Default NULL (auto). velocity_density numeric value velocity kernel density bandwidth. Default 1. velocity_smooth numeric value velocity smoothing parameter. Default 0.5. velocity_scale numeric value scaling velocity arrows. Default 1. velocity_min_mass numeric value minimum cell mass threshold velocity grid computation. Default 1. velocity_cutoff_perc numeric value velocity cutoff percentage. Default 5. velocity_group_palette character string naming palette velocity group colours (used "raw" plot type). Default "Set2". velocity_group_palcolor character vector specific colours velocity groups. Default NULL (auto-resolved velocity_group_palette). arrow_angle numeric value specifying angle arrowheads degrees. Applied arrow plot_type "raw" "grid". Default 20. arrow_color character string specifying color velocity arrows. plot_type = "stream", sets arrowhead color. Default "black". arrow_alpha numeric value 0 1 specifying transparency velocity arrows. used plot_type = "raw" "grid"; plot_type = "stream", use streamline_alpha instead. Default 1. streamline_l numeric value specifying integration length streamlines. Passed geom_streamline L parameter. Default 5. streamline_minl numeric value specifying minimum streamline length. Shorter streamlines drawn. Passed geom_streamline min.L parameter. Default 1. streamline_res numeric value specifying resolution streamline integration. Passed geom_streamline res parameter. Default 1. streamline_n numeric value specifying number streamlines draw. Passed geom_streamline n parameter. Default 15. streamline_width numeric vector length 2 specifying range line widths streamlines. Passed scale_size(range = ...). used streamline_color NULL. Default c(0, 0.8). streamline_alpha numeric value 0 1 specifying transparency velocity streamlines. Default 1. streamline_color optional character string specifying fixed color streamlines. NULL (default), streamlines colored velocity magnitude using streamline_palette. streamline_palette character string specifying color palette streamline velocity magnitude. Passed palette_this. used streamline_color NULL. Default "RdYlBu". streamline_palcolor optional character vector specific colors streamline velocity gradient. NULL, colors generated streamline_palette. Default NULL. streamline_bg_color character string specifying background (outline) color applied streamlines create stroke effect. Default "white". streamline_bg_stroke numeric value specifying additional line width background stroke relative foreground streamline. Default 0.5. keep_na logical value character replace NA values data. can also take named list specify different behavior different columns. TRUE NA, NA values replaced NA. FALSE, NA values removed data plotting. character string provided, NA values replaced provided string. named vector/list provided, names column names apply behavior , values one TRUE, FALSE, character string. Without named vector/list, behavior applies categorical/character columns used plot, example, x, group_by, fill_by, etc. keep_empty One FALSE, TRUE "level". can also take named list specify different behavior different columns. Without named list, behavior applies categorical/character columns used plot, example, x, group_by, fill_by, etc. FALSE (default): Drop empty factor levels data plotting. TRUE: Keep empty factor levels show separate category plot. "level": Keep empty factor levels, show plot. assigned colors palette maintain consistency across multiple plots. Alias: levels facet_by character string specifying column name data frame facet plot. Otherwise, data split split_by generate multiple plots combine one using patchwork::wrap_plots facet_scales Whether scale axes facets. Default "fixed" options "free", "free_x", "free_y". See ggplot2::facet_wrap facet_nrow numeric value specifying number rows facet. facet_by single column facet_wrap used. facet_ncol numeric value specifying number columns facet. facet_by single column facet_wrap used. facet_byrow logical value indicating whether fill plots row. Default TRUE. title character string specifying title plot. function can used generate title based default title. useful split_by used title needs dynamic. subtitle character string specifying subtitle plot. xlab character string specifying x-axis label. ylab character string specifying y-axis label. theme character string theme class (.e. ggplot2::theme_classic) specifying theme use. Default "theme_this". theme_args list arguments pass theme function. aspect.ratio numeric value specifying aspect ratio plot. legend.position character string specifying position legend. waiver(), single groups, legend "none", otherwise "right". legend.direction character string specifying direction legend. raster logical value. TRUE, points rendered via scattermore::geom_scattermore() efficient rasterised plotting. Default NULL, auto-enables nrow(data) > 1e5. raster_dpi numeric vector length 2 [x_dpi, y_dpi] specifying raster resolution pixels. Passed scattermore::geom_scattermore(pixels = ...). Default c(512, 512). single value provided recycled dimensions. hex logical value. TRUE, points rendered hexagonal bins via geom_hex() / stat_summary_hex(). supported highlight. Default FALSE. supported 2D. hex_linewidth numeric value width hexagon boundary lines. Default 0.5. hex_count logical value. TRUE group_by set, hex fill alpha mapped after_stat(count) denser bins opaque. features mode hex_count ignored. Default !.null(group_by). hex_bins numeric value number hex bins along axis. Passed geom_hex(bins = ...). Default 50. hex_binwidth numeric value width individual hex bins. Passed geom_hex(binwidth = ...). Takes precedence hex_bins set. palette character string specifying palette use. named list vector can used specify palettes different split_by values. palcolor character string specifying color use palette. named list can used specify colors different split_by values. values missing, values palette used (palcolor NULL values). palreverse logical value indicating whether reverse palette. Default FALSE. seed random seed use. Default 8525. combine Whether combine plots one facet FALSE. Default TRUE. nrow numeric value specifying number rows facet. ncol numeric value specifying number columns facet. byrow logical value indicating whether fill plots row. axes string specifying axes treated. Passed patchwork::wrap_plots(). relevant split_by used combine TRUE. Options : 'keep' retain axes individual plots. 'collect' remove duplicated axes placed run rows columns layout. 'collect_x' 'collect_y' remove duplicated x-axes columns duplicated y-axes rows respectively. axis_titles string specifying axis titltes treated. Passed patchwork::wrap_plots(). relevant split_by used combine TRUE. Options : 'keep' retain axis titles individual plots. 'collect' remove duplicated titles one direction merge titles opposite direction. 'collect_x' 'collect_y' control x-axis titles y-axis titles respectively. guides string specifying guides treated layout. Passed patchwork::wrap_plots(). relevant split_by used combine TRUE. Options : 'collect' collect guides given nesting level, removing duplicates. 'keep' stop collection level let guides placed alongside plot. 'auto' allow guides collected upper level tries, place alongside plot . design Specification location areas layout, passed patchwork::wrap_plots(). relevant split_by used combine TRUE. specified, nrow, ncol, byrow ignored. See patchwork::wrap_plots() details. ... Additional arguments. features character vector column names plot features (continuous colouring). multiple features provided facet_by set, data pivoted long format faceted feature name. lower_quantile, upper_quantile Lower upper quantiles continuous color/fill scale. actual cutoffs determined quantiles lower_cutoff upper_cutoff NULL. Defaults: lower_quantile = 0, upper_quantile = 0.99. lower_cutoff, upper_cutoff Explicit lower upper cutoffs continuous color/fill scale. NULL (default), cutoffs determined lower_quantile upper_quantile via quantile. Values outside [lower_cutoff, upper_cutoff] range clamped (winsorized) nearest cutoff value. bg_cutoff numeric threshold. Feature values absolute value cutoff set NA (therefore rendered bg_color). Default NULL. color_name character string used title continuous colour bar feature mode. Default "".

title: DimPlot / FeatureDimPlot — DimPlot
text: ggplot object (single plot), patchwork / wrap_plots object (split_by provided combine = TRUE), list ggplot objects (split_by provided combine = FALSE). dims 3 elements, plotly object returned instead. ggplot object (single plot), patchwork / wrap_plots object (split_by provided combine = TRUE), list ggplot objects (split_by provided combine = FALSE). dims 3 elements, plotly object returned instead.

title: DimPlot / FeatureDimPlot — DimPlot
text: split_by specified, DimPlot() executes following pipeline: Argument validation — validate_common_args() checks seed blocks split_by + velocity combinations. NA / empty normalisation — check_keep_na() / check_keep_empty() convert keep_na / keep_empty per-column lists. Theme resolution — process_theme() resolves theme string theme function. Split column resolution — check_columns() validates split_by (force_factor, concat_multi). Pre-filtering — process_keep_na_empty() removes NA / empty levels split column, data split split_by levels (order preserved). graph references attribute (@graph), graph matrix also subset per split. Per-split parameter resolution — check_palette(), check_palcolor(), check_legend() resolve palette, palcolor, legend.position, legend.direction split. Per-split dispatch — split passed DimPlotAtomic() resolved parameters. Title defaults split level name unless title function. Combination — combine_plots() assembles list plots via patchwork::wrap_plots(), applying nrow, ncol, byrow, axes, axis_titles, guides, design.

title: DimPlot / FeatureDimPlot — DimPlot
text: FeatureDimPlot() supports two forms splitting: . split_by = TRUE (split features) feature features dispatched individually DimPlotAtomic(), producing one plot per feature. plot title defaults feature name. Plots combined via combine_plots() split_by = ".features". B. split_by column name (split data column) Data split named column's levels (pipeline DimPlot steps 1–8 ). Graph attribute splitting supported.

what: Compute velocity on a regular grid from sparse cell embeddings — .compute_velocity_on_grid
title: Compute velocity on a regular grid from sparse cell embeddings — .compute_velocity_on_grid
text: Computes velocity vectors regular grid averaging cell-level velocities within Gaussian-weighted neighborhood grid point. function adapted scvelo Python implementation https://github.com/theislab/scvelo/blob/master/scvelo/plotting/velocity_embedding_grid.py.

title: Compute velocity on a regular grid from sparse cell embeddings — .compute_velocity_on_grid
code: .compute_velocity_on_grid(   embedding,   v_embedding,   density = NULL,   smooth = NULL,   n_neighbors = NULL,   min_mass = NULL,   scale = 1,   adjust_for_stream = FALSE,   cutoff_perc = NULL )

title: Compute velocity on a regular grid from sparse cell embeddings — .compute_velocity_on_grid
text: embedding numeric matrix dimension n_obs x n_dim containing low-dimensional embedding coordinates cell. v_embedding numeric matrix dimension n_obs x n_dim containing velocity vectors cell. density numeric value specifying density grid points along dimension. Higher values produce finer grid. Default 1. smooth numeric value specifying standard deviation multiplier Gaussian kernel used weight neighboring cells averaging velocities onto grid points. Default 0.5. n_neighbors integer value specifying number nearest neighbors consider grid point computing weighted average velocity. Default ceiling(n_obs / 50). min_mass numeric value specifying minimum mass threshold. Grid points total weight threshold filtered . adjust_for_stream = TRUE, interpreted logarithmic scale (10^(min_mass - 6)). Default 1. scale numeric value specifying scaling factor apply resulting grid velocity vectors. used adjust_for_stream = FALSE. Default 1. adjust_for_stream logical value indicating whether adjust output streamline rendering. TRUE, returns 2D array format different filtering logic suitable metR::geom_streamline. Default FALSE. cutoff_perc numeric value specifying percentile cutoff removing low-density grid points. used adjust_for_stream = TRUE. Default 5.

title: Compute velocity on a regular grid from sparse cell embeddings — .compute_velocity_on_grid
text: list components x_grid (grid point coordinates) v_grid (velocity vectors grid point). adjust_for_stream = TRUE, x_grid 2-row matrix unique coordinates v_grid 3D array.

what: Dot Plot, Scatter Plot, and Lollipop Plot — DotPlot
title: Dot Plot, Scatter Plot, and Lollipop Plot — DotPlot
text: DotPlot() renders matrix filled circles (dot plot) dot size encodes one numeric variable fill colour encodes another. Either axis can numeric factor, enabling four layout combinations: axes factor — classic dot matrix (e.g. genes × cell types), cell dot whose size reflects expression magnitude whose colour reflects summary statistic. axes numeric — scatter plot, dots positioned x/y coordinates, sized third variable, coloured fourth. One numeric, one factor — strip plot (lollipop = TRUE) lollipop chart. LollipopPlot() convenience wrapper sets lollipop = TRUE, producing horizontal bars y-axis data point, capped filled dots. expects numeric x factor/character y. Key features: Auto-count: size_by = NULL, per-combination observation count computed automatically. fill_cutoff: values fill_by matching threshold expression (e.g. "< 18") greyed dedicated legend entry. Background stripes: add_bg = TRUE draws alternating background bands along discrete axis visual grouping. Border modes: border_color can track fill gradient (TRUE), use constant colour ("black"), suppressed (FALSE). Colour scale trimming: lower_quantile / upper_quantile (explicit lower_cutoff / upper_cutoff) trim continuous fill scale extremes. LollipopPlot() convenience wrapper around DotPlot() sets lollipop = TRUE. renders horizontal bar extending y-axis (x = 0) data point, capped filled dot. bar two-layer construction: outer shadow (black custom colour) inner coloured segment follows fill_by gradient. Dot size scales size_by (per-combination observation count size_by = NULL). Expects x numeric column y factor character column.

title: Dot Plot, Scatter Plot, and Lollipop Plot — DotPlot
text: data data frame. x character string naming column x-axis. Must numeric column (bars extend 0 data value). y character string naming column y-axis. Must factor character column (level gets lollipop bar). x_sep character string used join multiple x column values single factor level. used x non-numeric multiple columns provided. Default: "_". y_sep character string used join multiple y column values single factor level. used y non-numeric multiple columns provided. Default: "_". flip logical value. TRUE, x y axes swapped via coord_flip(). Dimension calculation accounts flip. Default: FALSE. split_by column(s) split data generate separate plots level. split column processed keep_na / keep_empty splitting. split_by_sep character string used concatenate multiple split_by column values. Default: "_". size_name character string size legend title. NULL (default), size_by column name used. fill_name character string fill colour-bar legend title. NULL (default), fill_by column name used. fill_cutoff_name character string fill cutoff legend title (shown fill_cutoff active).
