title: Dot Plot, Scatter Plot, and Lollipop Plot — DotPlot
text: ggplot object (single plot), patchwork object (combine = TRUE split_by), list ggplot objects (combine = FALSE). ggplot object (single plot), patchwork object (combine = TRUE split_by), list ggplot objects (combine = FALSE).

title: Dot Plot, Scatter Plot, and Lollipop Plot — DotPlot
text: split_by provided, following pipeline executes: Column validation — check_columns() resolves split_by (force_factor, allow_multi, concat_multi). NA / empty pre-processing — process_keep_na_empty() handles keep_na / keep_empty split column splitting, removes split column per-split keep_na/keep_empty lists. Data splitting — splits data split_by levels (preserving factor level order). Per-split palette / colour — check_palette() check_palcolor() resolve per-split palette colour overrides. Per-split legend — check_legend() resolves legend.position legend.direction per split. Per-split title — title function, receives default title (split level name) can return custom string; otherwise title %||% split_level used. Dispatch — split subset passed DotPlotAtomic (lollipop = FALSE). Combination — combine_plots() assembles list plots via patchwork::wrap_plots, honouring nrow/ncol/byrow/design.

title: Dot Plot, Scatter Plot, and Lollipop Plot — DotPlot
text: pipeline DotPlot , dispatches DotPlotAtomic lollipop = TRUE.

title: Dot Plot, Scatter Plot, and Lollipop Plot — DotPlot
code: # \donttest{ mtcars <- datasets::mtcars mtcars$carb <- factor(mtcars$carb) mtcars$gear <- factor(mtcars$gear)  # --- Basic dot plot (factor × factor, size + fill) --- DotPlot(mtcars, x = "carb", y = "gear", size_by = "wt",         fill_by = "mpg", fill_cutoff = "< 18")  DotPlot(mtcars, x = "carb", y = "gear", size_by = "wt",         fill_by = "mpg", fill_cutoff = "> 18")   # --- Background stripes --- DotPlot(mtcars, x = "carb", y = "gear", size_by = "wt",         fill_by = "mpg", fill_cutoff = "< 18", add_bg = TRUE)  DotPlot(mtcars, x = "carb", y = "gear", size_by = "wt",         fill_by = "mpg", fill_cutoff = "< 18", add_bg = TRUE,         bg_direction = "h")   # --- Faceting --- DotPlot(mtcars, x = "carb", y = "gear", size_by = "wt",         fill_by = "mpg", fill_cutoff = "< 18", facet_by = "cyl")  DotPlot(mtcars, x = "carb", y = "gear", size_by = "wt",         fill_by = "mpg", fill_cutoff = "< 18", facet_by = "cyl",         facet_scales = "free_x")   # --- split_by --- DotPlot(mtcars, x = "carb", y = "gear", size_by = "wt",         fill_by = "mpg", fill_cutoff = "< 18", split_by = "cyl")  DotPlot(mtcars, x = "carb", y = "gear", size_by = "wt",         fill_by = "mpg", fill_cutoff = "< 18", split_by = "cyl",         palette = list("4" = "Set1", "6" = "Paired", "8" = "Reds"))   # --- Scatter plot (both axes numeric) --- DotPlot(mtcars, x = "qsec", y = "drat", size_by = "wt",         fill_by = "mpg", fill_cutoff = "< 18",         fill_cutoff_name = "Small mpgs")   # --- keep_na and keep_empty --- mtcars$carb[mtcars$carb == "1"] <- NA mtcars$gear[mtcars$gear == "3"] <- NA DotPlot(mtcars, x = "carb", y = "gear", size_by = "wt",         fill_by = "mpg", fill_cutoff = "< 18",         keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = TRUE)   # --- Border customization --- DotPlot(mtcars, x = "carb", y = "gear", size_by = "wt",         fill_by = "mpg", border_color = "red", border_size = 2)  DotPlot(mtcars, x = "carb", y = "gear", size_by = "wt",         fill_by = "mpg", border_color = TRUE, border_size = 1.5,         border_alpha = 0.5)  DotPlot(mtcars, x = "carb", y = "gear",         fill_by = "mpg", border_color = FALSE) #> Warning: [DotPlot] Using the first value of fill_by.   # --- Colour scale trimming --- DotPlot(mtcars, x = "carb", y = "gear", size_by = "wt",         fill_by = "mpg", lower_quantile = 0.05, upper_quantile = 0.95)  DotPlot(mtcars, x = "carb", y = "gear", size_by = "wt",         fill_by = "mpg", lower_cutoff = 15, upper_cutoff = 25)  # } # \donttest{ mtcars <- datasets::mtcars  # --- Basic lollipop --- LollipopPlot(mtcars, x = "qsec", y = "drat", size_by = "wt",              fill_by = "mpg")   # --- Faceting --- LollipopPlot(mtcars, x = "qsec", y = "drat", size_by = "wt",              fill_by = "mpg", fill_cutoff = "< 18", facet_by = "cyl",              facet_scales = "free_y")   # --- split_by --- LollipopPlot(mtcars, x = "qsec", y = "drat", size_by = "wt",              split_by = "vs", palette = list("0" = "Reds", "1" = "Blues"))   # --- Border customization --- LollipopPlot(mtcars, x = "qsec", y = "drat", size_by = "wt",              fill_by = "mpg", border_color = "red", border_size = 2)  LollipopPlot(mtcars, x = "qsec", y = "drat", size_by = "wt",              fill_by = "mpg", border_color = TRUE, border_size = 1.5,              border_alpha = 0.5)  LollipopPlot(mtcars, x = "qsec", y = "drat",              fill_by = "mpg", border_color = FALSE) #> Warning: [DotPlot] Using the first value of fill_by.  # }

what: Enrichment Map and Enrichment Network — EnrichMap
title: Enrichment Map and Enrichment Network — EnrichMap
text: EnrichMap draws enrichment map – gene-set similarity network node enriched term, node size encodes number associated genes, node fill colour encodes cluster membership (detected via igraph community detection), edge thickness encodes number overlapping genes term pairs.  plot uses force-directed layout arrange terms, ggforce hull annotations group terms clusters.  Keyword term-description labels appear legend. EnrichNetwork draws enrichment network – term-gene bipartite graph term nodes shown numbered circles gene nodes labelled rectangles.  Gene node colours blended colours terms belong .  force-directed layout positions nodes, optional overlap adjustment better readability. functions accept enrichment results clusterProfiler Enrichr (latter auto-detected preprocessed via prepare_enrichr_result()).

title: Enrichment Map and Enrichment Network — EnrichMap
code: EnrichMap(   data,   in_form = c("auto", "clusterProfiler", "clusterprofiler", "enrichr"),   split_by = NULL,   split_by_sep = "_",   top_term = 10,   metric = "p.adjust",   layout = "fr",   minchar = 2,   cluster = "fast_greedy",   show_keyword = FALSE,   nlabel = 4,   character_width = 50,   mark = "ellipse",   label = c("term", "feature"),   labelsize = 5,   expand = c(0.4, 0.4),   theme = "theme_this",   theme_args = list(),   palette = "Paired",   palcolor = NULL,   palreverse = FALSE,   alpha = 1,   aspect.ratio = 1,   legend.position = "right",   legend.direction = "vertical",   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   seed = 8525,   combine = TRUE,   nrow = NULL,   ncol = NULL,   byrow = TRUE,   axes = NULL,   axis_titles = axes,   guides = NULL,   design = NULL,   ... )  EnrichNetwork(   data,   in_form = c("auto", "clusterProfiler", "clusterprofiler", "enrichr"),   split_by = NULL,   split_by_sep = "_",   top_term = 10,   metric = "p.adjust",   character_width = 50,   layout = "fr",   layoutadjust = TRUE,   adjscale = 60,   adjiter = 100,   blendmode = "blend",   labelsize = 5,   theme = "theme_this",   theme_args = list(),   palette = "Paired",   palcolor = NULL,   palreverse = FALSE,   alpha = 1,   aspect.ratio = 1,   legend.position = "right",   legend.direction = "vertical",   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   xlab = NULL,   ylab = NULL,   seed = 8525,   combine = TRUE,   nrow = NULL,   ncol = NULL,   byrow = TRUE,   axes = NULL,   axis_titles = axes,   guides = NULL,   design = NULL,   ... )

Default "fr". minchar integer specifying minimum character length words included keywords show_keyword = TRUE. FALSE (default), original term descriptions used labels. nlabel integer specifying number keywords term descriptions show per cluster legend labels. Either "term" (default; shows top term descriptions/keywords per cluster) "feature" (shows top gene symbols per cluster). labelsize numeric value specifying font size cluster labels drawn ggforce mark layer.  Default 5. expand values expand x y axes. like CSS padding. single value provided, used axes sides. two values provided, first value used top/bottom side second value used left/right side. three values provided, first value used top side, second value used left/right side, third value used bottom side. four values provided, values used top, right, bottom, left sides, respectively. can also use named vector specify values side. axis discrete, values applied 'add' 'expansion' function. axis continuous, values applied 'mult' 'expansion' function. Default FALSE. alpha numeric value specifying transparency plot. aspect.ratio numeric value specifying aspect ratio plot. legend.position character string specifying position legend. waiver(), single groups, legend "none", otherwise "right". legend.direction character string specifying direction legend. title character string specifying title plot. function can used generate title based default title. useful split_by used title needs dynamic. subtitle character string specifying subtitle plot. xlab character string specifying x-axis label. ylab character string specifying y-axis label. seed random seed use. Default 8525. combine Whether combine plots one facet FALSE. TRUE (default), applies adjust_network_layout() initial layout reduce node overlap based label width repulsion simulation. adjscale numeric value controlling scale layout adjustment. title: Enrichment Map and Enrichment Network — EnrichMap
text: split_by provided, EnrichMap() executes following pipeline: Argument validation – validate_common_args() checks seed. Input format detection – match.arg() resolves in_form; "auto" mode infers format column names. Enrichr preprocessing – format "enrichr", calls prepare_enrichr_result() rename columns infer GeneRatio/BgRatio. Split column resolution – check_columns() validates split_by (force_factor, allow_multi, concat_multi). Data splitting – splits data split_by levels, preserving factor level order. Per-split palette/colour – check_palette() check_palcolor() resolve per-split palette colour overrides. Per-split legend – check_legend() resolves legend.position legend.direction per split. Per-split title – title function, receives default title (split level name); otherwise title %||% split_level used. Dispatch – split subset passed EnrichMapAtomic resolved parameters. Combination – combine_plots() assembles list plots via patchwork::wrap_plots, honouring nrow/ncol/byrow/axes/ axis_titles/guides/design.

title: Enrichment Map and Enrichment Network — EnrichMap

title: Prepare cutoff data for ROC curve annotation — get_cutoffs_data
text: Internal helper computes sensitivity specificity values set user-supplied cutoff points (computed via OptimalCutpoints methods) across categories defined group_by /facet_by columns. Used ROCCurveAtomic() annotate ROC curve cutoff markers labels.

title: Prepare cutoff data for ROC curve annotation — get_cutoffs_data
code: get_cutoffs_data(   data,   truth_by,   score_by,   cat_by,   cutoffs_at = NULL,   cutoffs_labels = NULL,   cutoffs_accuracy = 0.001,   n_cuts = 0,   increasing = TRUE )

title: Prepare cutoff data for ROC curve annotation — get_cutoffs_data
text: data data frame truth score columns. truth_by character string column name contains true class labels (binary, 0/1 TRUE/FALSE). score_by character string column name contains predicted scores. cat_by character string column name categorise/group data. specified, cutoffs calculated separately category level, enabling per-group per-facet cutoff annotation. cutoffs_at vector user-supplied cutoff values plot points. non-NULL, overrides n_cuts. Supports raw numeric values method names optimal.cutpoints. cutoffs_labels character vector user-supplied labels cutoffs. Must length cutoffs_at. NULL, labels generated automatically. cutoffs_accuracy numeric value specifying rounding precision automatically generated cutoff labels. Default: 0.001. n_cuts integer specifying number evenly-spaced quantile-based cutoff points. Ignored cutoffs_at non-NULL. Default: 0 (quantile cutoffs). increasing logical value. TRUE (default), higher scores indicate positive class; FALSE, lower scores indicate positive class.

title: Prepare cutoff data for ROC curve annotation — get_cutoffs_data

title: Prepare cutoff data for ROC curve annotation — get_cutoffs_data

title: GSEA summary dot plot — GSEASummaryPlot
text: Produces summary dot plot GSEA (Gene Set Enrichment Analysis) results. row represents gene set (term), positioned along x-axis Normalized Enrichment Score (NES). Dot colour encodes significance level (typically -log10(p.adjust)) continuous gradient, row includes miniature line plot showing gene ranks running enrichment score term's gene set. function supports DOSE fgsea package output formats via in_form parameter. Terms can ranked selected significance metric (top_term, metric), non-significant terms rendered grey. per-term line plots can show either raw preranked gene statistics (line_by = "prerank") running enrichment score (line_by = "running_score").

title: GSEA summary dot plot — GSEASummaryPlot

title: Jitter plot — JitterPlot
text: Draws jittered point plot showing distribution numeric y-values across discrete x-axis.  data point rendered random jitter along x-axis (optionally y-axis) reduce overplotting, making easy visualise data density, spread, outliers within category. function supports x-axis reordering y-value summaries (mean median), group dodging via group_by compare subgroups side--side, point labelling automatic top-n selection using configurable distance metric (default: radial distance y^2 + size^2), point highlighting emphasis, optional horizontal reference lines, wide-format input via in_form.  Colour control, faceting, splitting separate sub-plots via split_by supported.

title: Jitter plot — JitterPlot
text: split_by provided: validate_common_args() validates seed. check_keep_na() check_keep_empty() normalise keep_na / keep_empty arguments relevant columns (x, split_by, group_by, facet_by). split_by column validated via check_columns() force_factor = TRUE. Per-split palette, palcolor, legend.position, legend.direction resolved via check_palette(), check_palcolor(), check_legend(). title: Jitter plot — JitterPlot
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(8525) n <- 180 x <- factor(     sample(c("A", NA, LETTERS[3:5]), n, replace = TRUE),     levels = c("A", "B", "C", "D", "E") ) group <- factor(     sample(c("G1", NA, "G3"), n, replace = TRUE),     levels = c("G1", "G2", "G3") ) size <- rexp(n, rate = 1) id <- paste0("pt", seq_len(n)) y <- rnorm(n, mean = ifelse(is.na(group), 0, ifelse(group == "G1", 0.5, -0.5))) +      as.numeric(ifelse(is.na(x), 0, x))/10 df <- data.frame(   x = x,   y = y,   group = group,   size = size,   id = id )  # Basic JitterPlot(df, x = "x", y = "y")   # Keep empty x levels and NA JitterPlot(df, x = "x", y = "y", keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = TRUE)   # Map size with transform; legend shows original values JitterPlot(df, x = "x", y = "y", size_by = "size", size_name = "Abundance",     size_trans = sqrt, order_by = "-y^2")   # Dodge by group and add a horizontal line JitterPlot(df, x = "x", y = "y", group_by = "group",   add_hline = 0, hline_type = "dashed", hline_color = "red2")   # Keep the empty levels only for color coding # Note the G3 is not blue (which is taken by unused level G2) JitterPlot(df, x = "x", y = "y", group_by = "group",     keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = 'level')   # Label top points by distance (y^2 + size^2) JitterPlot(df, x = "x", y = "y", size_by = "size", label_by = "id", nlabel = 3)   # Flip axes JitterPlot(df, x = "x", y = "y", flip = TRUE)  # }

"type") <- "discrete"       } else {         attr(palcolor, "type") <- "continuous"       }     } else if (pal %in% names(ggsci_db)) {       if (pal %in% c("d3", "uchicago", "material")) {         for (subpal in names(ggsci_db[[pal]])) {           palcolor <- ggsci_db[[pal]][[subpal]]           if (pal == "material") {             attr(palcolor, "type") <- "continuous"           } else { if (pal == "gsea") {           attr(palcolor, "type") <- "continuous"         } else {           attr(palcolor, "type") <- "discrete"         }       }     } else if (pal %in% rownames(redmonder.pal.info)) {       pal_n <- redmonder.pal.info[pal, "maxcolors"]       pal_category <- redmonder.pal.info[pal, "category"]       if (pal_category == "div") {         palcolor <- rev(redmonder.pal(name = pal, n = pal_n))       } else {         palcolor <- redmonder.pal(name = pal, n = pal_n)       }       if (pal_category == "qual") {         attr(palcolor, "type") <- "discrete"       } else {         attr(palcolor, "type") <- "continuous"       }     } else if (pal %in% rownames(metacartocolors)) {       pal_n <- metacartocolors[pal, "Max_n"]       palcolor <- carto_pal(name = pal, n = pal_n)       if (pal_category == "qualitative") {         attr(palcolor, "type") <- "discrete"       } else {         attr(palcolor, "type") <- "continuous"       }     } else if (pal %in% names(nord_palettes)) {       palcolor <- nord_palettes[[pal]]       attr(palcolor, "type") <- "discrete"     } else if (pal %in% names(viridis_palettes)) {       palcolor <- viridis_palettes[[pal]]       attr(palcolor, "type") <- "continuous"     } else if (pal %in% names(ocean_palettes)) {       palcolor <- ocean_palettes[[pal]]       attr(palcolor, "type") <- "continuous"     } else if (pal %in% names(dichromat_palettes)) {       palcolor <- dichromat_palettes[[pal]]       if (pal %in% c("Categorical.12", "SteppedSequential.5")) {         attr(palcolor, "type") <- "discrete"       } else {         attr(palcolor, "type") <- "continuous"       }     } else if (pal %in% jcolors_names) {       palcolor <- jcolors(palette = gsub("jcolors-", "", pal))       if (pal %in% paste0("jcolors-", c("pal10", "pal11", "pal12", "rainbow"))) {         attr(palcolor, "type") <- "continuous"       } else {         attr(palcolor, "type") <- "discrete"       }     } else if (pal %in% custom_names) {       palcolor <- custom_palettes[[pal]]       if (pal %in% c("jet")) {         attr(palcolor, "type") <- "continuous"       } else {         attr(palcolor, "type") <- "discrete"       }     } else if (pal %in% names(seurat_discrete_palettes)) {       palcolor <- seurat_discrete_palettes[[pal]]       attr(palcolor, "type") <- "discrete"     } else if (pal %in% names(seurat_continuous_palettes)) {       palcolor <- seurat_continuous_palettes[[pal]]       attr(palcolor, "type") <- "continuous"     } else if (pal %in% names(stripe_palettes)) {       palcolor <- stripe_palettes[[pal]]       attr(palcolor, "type") <- "discrete"     } else if (pal %in% names(tableau_palettes)) {       palcolor <- tableau_palettes[[pal]]       attr(palcolor, "type") <- "discrete"     }     palette_list[[pal]] <- palcolor   } } # }

title: Radar plot / Spider plot — RadarPlot
text: Draws radar chart (concentric circular grid) spider chart (polygonal grid) displaying multivariate data two-dimensional polar coordinate system.  x-axis category placed evenly spaced angular position around chart, numeric values plotted along radial axis. function supports count aggregation (omit y plot observation counts), proportion scaling (via scale_y), per-group colour control, faceting, splitting separate sub-plots via split_by. SpiderPlot alias renders data polygonal grid lines (spider chart style) using polygon = TRUE. variant RadarPlot renders chart straight polygonal grid lines (spider chart) instead concentric circles.  Internally, calls RadarPlotAtomic polygon = TRUE otherwise identical RadarPlot behaviour parameters.

title: Radar plot / Spider plot — RadarPlot
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(8525)  # --- Radar chart with observation counts --- data <- data.frame(     x = factor(         c(rep("A", 20), rep("B", 30), rep(NA, 30), rep("D", 40), rep("E", 50)),         levels = LETTERS[1:5]     ),     group = factor(         sample(c("G1", NA, "G3", "G4"), 170, replace = TRUE),         levels = c("G1", "G2", "G3", "G4")     ) )  # Basic radar chart RadarPlot(data, x = "x")   # Keep NA and empty factor levels RadarPlot(data, x = "x", keep_na = TRUE, keep_empty = TRUE)   # Custom background colour RadarPlot(data, x = "x", bg_color = "lightpink")   # Raw counts (no proportion scaling) RadarPlot(data, x = "x", scale_y = "none")   # Grouped by a variable RadarPlot(data, x = "x", group_by = "group", keep_na = TRUE)   # Faceted by a variable RadarPlot(data, x = "x", facet_by = "group")   # Spider chart variant (polygonal grid) SpiderPlot(data, x = "x")  SpiderPlot(data, x = "x", group_by = "group")   # --- Radar chart with explicit y values --- data <- data.frame(     x = rep(LETTERS[1:5], 2),     y = c(1, 3, 6, 4, 2, 5, 7, 8, 9, 10),     group = rep(c("G1", "G2"), each = 5) )  # Grouped radar with raw values RadarPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", scale_y = "none", group_by = "group")   # Faceted radar RadarPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", facet_by = "group")   # Split into separate sub-plots RadarPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", split_by = "group")   # Per-split palettes RadarPlot(data, x = "x", y = "y", split_by = "group",           palette = c(G1 = "Set1", G2 = "Paired"))  # }

title: Sankey / Alluvial Plot — SankeyPlot
text: Draws Sankey (alluvial) diagrams visualise flow, movement, change one categorical state another across discrete positions (time points, stages, groups).  plot consists nodes (vertical blocks, strata) representing categories position, links (alluvia / flows) representing observation units move categories across positions. function accepts data several formats, controlled in_form: "lodes" / "long" row observation one x-position, columns x, stratum, alluvium, optionally y. "alluvia" / "wide" row observation unit tracked across positions; x columns represent categories position.  Converted internally via to_lodes_form(). "counts" Numeric columns x represent frequencies.  first element x ".", links_fill_by values injected additional first column nodes, visualising source distribution flows. "auto" (default) Automatically detects format: numeric multi-column x → "counts"; multi-column x passing is_alluvia_form → "alluvia"; otherwise → "lodes". Supports split_by produce separate sub-plots different subsets data, facet_by within-plot faceting, independent styling nodes links (colours, alpha, borders, labels, legend behaviour). AlluvialPlot alias SankeyPlot.

See data parameter SankeyPlot format descriptions. x character string specifying column name data frame plot x-axis. x_sep character string join multiple x columns in_form "lodes" auto-determined lodes. NULL, defaults links_fill_by.  Multiple columns concatenated stratum_sep.  Ignored "alluvia" format. stratum_sep character string join multiple stratum columns.  Default "_". alluvium character string specifying column identifies individual flows (alluvia) across x-axis positions.  unique value represents single observational unit tracked across positions.  NULL "counts" format, auto-generated identifier created.  Multiple columns concatenated alluvium_sep. Ignored "alluvia" format. alluvium_sep character string join multiple alluvium columns. TRUE: Keep empty factor levels show separate category plot. "level": Keep empty factor levels, show plot. assigned colors palette maintain consistency across multiple plots. Alias: levels flow logical value.  FALSE (default), geom_alluvium() used links.  TRUE, geom_flow() used instead, draws flows directional gradient x positions. expand values expand x y axes. like CSS padding. single value provided, used axes sides. two values provided, first value used top/bottom side second value used left/right side. three values provided, first value used top side, second value used left/right side, third value used bottom side. four values provided, values used top, right, bottom, left sides, respectively. can also use named vector specify values side. axis discrete, values applied 'add' 'expansion' function. axis continuous, values applied 'mult' 'expansion' function. See also https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/expansion.html nodes_legend Controls node legend displayed.  One : "auto" (default) Automatically determined: nodes_label = TRUE, stratum identical links_fill_by matching colours, legend hidden. Otherwise, overlapping stratum values across x positions checked: overlap produces merged legend; overlap produces separate legends per x position. "merge" single merged legend nodes. "separate" One legend per x-axis position, generated via separate scale_fill_manual() layers. "none" node legend shown. nodes_color character string specifying border colour node (stratum) rectangles.  Use special value ".fill" match border colour node fill colour.  Default "grey30". links_fill_by character string specifying column determines fill colour links (alluvia / flows).  NULL "lodes" format, defaults alluvium.  "counts" format "." prefix, parameter required.  Multiple columns concatenated links_fill_by_sep. links_fill_by_sep character string join multiple links_fill_by columns. Use special value ".fill" match link border colour link fill colour.  Default "gray80". nodes_palette character string specifying colour palette node (stratum) fill.  Passed palette_this(). code: # \donttest{ # Examples from ggalluvial datasets set.seed(8525)  data(UCBAdmissions, package = "datasets") UCBAdmissions <- as.data.frame(UCBAdmissions) SankeyPlot(as.data.frame(UCBAdmissions), x = c("Gender", "Dept"),     y = "Freq", nodes_width = 1/12, links_fill_by = "Admit", nodes_label = TRUE,     nodes_palette = "simspec", links_palette = "Set1", links_alpha = 0.5,     nodes_palcolor = "black", links_color = "transparent")   data(HairEyeColor, package = "datasets") SankeyPlot(as.data.frame(HairEyeColor), x = c("Hair", "Eye", "Sex"),     y = "Freq", links_fill_by = "Eye", nodes_width = 1/8, nodes_alpha = 0.4,     flip = TRUE, reverse = FALSE, knot.pos = 0, links_color = "transparent",     ylab = "Freq", links_alpha = 0.5, links_name = "Eye (links)", links_palcolor = c(         Brown = "#70493D", Hazel = "#E2AC76", Green = "#3F752B", Blue = "#81B0E4"))   data(Refugees, package = "alluvial") country_regions <- c(     Afghanistan = "Middle East",     Burundi = "Central Africa",     `Congo DRC` = "Central Africa",     Iraq = "Middle East",     Myanmar = "Southeast Asia",     Palestine = "Middle East",     Somalia = "Horn of Africa",     Sudan = "Central Africa",     Syria = "Middle East",     Vietnam = "Southeast Asia" ) Refugees$region <- country_regions[Refugees$country] SankeyPlot(Refugees, x = "year", y = "refugees", alluvium = "country",     links_fill_by = "country", links_color = ".fill", links_alpha = 0.75,     links_palette = "Set3", facet_by = "region", x_text_angle = -45, nodes_legend = "none",     theme_args = list(strip.background = ggplot2::element_rect(fill="grey80")),     decreasing = FALSE, nodes_width = 0, nodes_color = "transparent", ylab = "refugees",     title = "Refugee volume by country and region of origin")   data(majors, package = "ggalluvial") majors$curriculum <- as.factor(majors$curriculum) SankeyPlot(majors, x = "semester", stratum = "curriculum", alluvium = "student",     links_fill_by = "curriculum", flow = TRUE, stat = "alluvium", nodes_palette = "Set2",     links_palette = "Set2")   data(vaccinations, package = "ggalluvial") vaccinations <- transform(vaccinations,     response = factor(response, rev(levels(response)))) SankeyPlot(vaccinations, x = "survey", stratum = "response", alluvium = "subject",     y = "freq", links_fill_by = "response", nodes_label = TRUE, nodes_alpha = 0.5,     nodes_palette = "seurat", links_palette = "seurat", links_alpha = 0.5,     legend.position = "none", flow = TRUE, expand = c(0, 0, 0, .15), stat = "alluvium",     title = "vaccination survey responses at three points in time")   data(Titanic, package = "datasets") SankeyPlot(as.data.frame(Titanic), x = c("Class", "Sex"), y = "Freq",     links_fill_by = "Survived", flow = TRUE, facet_by = "Age", facet_scales = "free_y",     nodes_label = TRUE, expand = c(0.05, 0), xlab = "", links_palette = "Set1",     nodes_palcolor = "white", nodes_label_miny = 10)   # Simulated examples df <- data.frame(     Clone = paste0("clone", 1:10),     Timepoint1 = sample(c(rep(0, 30), 1:100), 10),     Timepoint2 = sample(c(rep(0, 30), 1:100), 10) ) SankeyPlot(df, x = c("Timepoint1", "Timepoint2"), alluvium = "Clone",     links_color = ".fill")   df <- data.frame(     Clone = rep(paste0("clone", 1:6), each = 2),     Timepoint1 = sample(c(rep(0, 30), 1:100), 6),     Timepoint2 = sample(c(rep(0, 30), 1:100), 6),     Group = rep(c("A", "B"), 6) ) SankeyPlot(df, x = c(".", "Timepoint1", "Timepoint2"),     stratum = "Group", links_fill_by = "Clone", links_color = ".fill")  # }

title: Show the color palettes — show_palettes

title: Show the color palettes — show_palettes

Default NULL. palette_names character vector specifying names SCP palettes include. Default NULL. return_names logical value indicating whether return names selected palettes. Default TRUE. return_palettes logical value indicating whether return colors selected palettes. Default FALSE.

what: Spatial visualization functions for terra objects and point data — SpatImagePlot
title: Spatial visualization functions for terra objects and point data — SpatImagePlot
text: functions provide publication-quality spatial visualizations built ggplot2, supporting raster images, categorical masks, vector shapes, point data terra package standard data frames.

title: Spatial visualization functions for terra objects and point data — SpatImagePlot
text: data SpatRaster, SpatVector, data.frame depending function. See individual function descriptions. ext numeric vector length 4 c(xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) terra::SpatExtent object. Default NULL (use full extent). raster Whether rasterize efficient rendering large datasets. Default NULL (auto-detect: rasterize ncell > 1e6 nrow > 1e6). Uses scattermore::geom_scattermore() points. raster_dpi numeric vector length 1 2 specifying raster output resolution pixels. Default c(512, 512). flip_y Whether negate y-coordinates axis labels can displayed reversed sign. Default TRUE. palette character string specifying palette use. named list vector can used specify palettes different split_by values. palcolor character string specifying color use palette. named list can used specify colors different split_by values. values missing, values palette used (palcolor NULL values). palreverse logical value indicating whether reverse palette. Default FALSE. alpha numeric value specifying transparency plot. fill_name character string fill legend title. return_layer Whether return list ggplot layers instead complete plot. Default FALSE. TRUE, returned list "scales" attribute layer conflict detection, allowing multiple spatial layers single custom ggplot. theme character string theme class (.e. ggplot2::theme_classic) specifying theme use. Default "theme_this". theme_args list arguments pass theme function. legend.position character string specifying position legend. waiver(), single groups, legend "none", otherwise "right". legend.direction character string specifying direction legend. title character string specifying title plot. function can used generate title based default title. useful split_by used title needs dynamic. subtitle character string specifying subtitle plot. xlab character string specifying x-axis label. ylab character string specifying y-axis label. seed random seed use. Default 8525. add_border Whether add polygon borders around mask regions SpatMasksPlot. Default TRUE. border_color character string border color. Default "black". TRUE SpatShapesPlot SpatPointsPlot, border maps variable fill. border_size numeric value border line width. Default 0.5. border_alpha numeric value border transparency. Default 1. x character string specifying x coordinate column SpatPointsPlot SpatShapesPlot (data data frame). Auto-detected common column names NULL. y character string specifying y coordinate column SpatPointsPlot SpatShapesPlot (data data frame). Auto-detected common column names NULL. group character string specifying grouping column SpatShapesPlot data data.frame. unique value column defines separate polygon. fill_by character string vector specifying column(s) map fill color SpatShapesPlot. multiple columns provided (must numeric), data reshaped faceted. single string match column name, treated fixed fill color. highlight character vector row names highlight, filter expression string (e.g., 'cat == ""'), TRUE (highlight ). Highlighted points overlaid larger markers. highlight_alpha numeric value highlight transparency. Default 1. highlight_size numeric value highlight marker size. Default 1. highlight_color character string highlight marker color. Default "black". highlight_stroke numeric value highlight stroke width (added border_size). Default 0.8. facet_scales Whether scale axes facets. Default "fixed" options "free", "free_x", "free_y". See ggplot2::facet_wrap facet_nrow numeric value specifying number rows facet. facet_by single column facet_wrap used. facet_ncol numeric value specifying number columns facet. facet_by single column facet_wrap used. facet_byrow logical value indicating whether fill plots row. Default TRUE. color_by character string vector specifying column(s) map point color SpatPointsPlot. Multiple numeric columns trigger faceting. size_by character string specifying column map point size SpatPointsPlot. Mutually exclusive size. size numeric value fixed point size SpatPointsPlot. Alias size_by given scalar. lower_quantile, upper_quantile Lower upper quantiles continuous color/fill scale. actual cutoffs determined quantiles lower_cutoff upper_cutoff NULL. Defaults: lower_quantile = 0, upper_quantile = 0.99. lower_cutoff, upper_cutoff Explicit lower upper cutoffs continuous color/fill scale. NULL (default), cutoffs determined lower_quantile upper_quantile via quantile. Values outside [lower_cutoff, upper_cutoff] range clamped (winsorized) nearest cutoff value. color_name character string color legend title. size_name character string size legend title. shape numeric value (21–25 border shapes) character string specifying point shape SpatPointsPlot. Default 16 (filled circle). hex Whether use hexagonal binning SpatPointsPlot. Requires numeric color_by. Default FALSE. hex_linewidth numeric value hex bin border width. Default 0.5. hex_count Whether show hex bin count point opacity. Default FALSE. hex_bins numeric value number hex bins. Default 50. hex_binwidth numeric value hex bin width. Default NULL. label Whether show group labels SpatPointsPlot. Default FALSE. Requires categorical color_by. label_size numeric value label text size. Default 4. label_fg character string label text color. Default "white". label_bg character string label background color. Default "black". label_bg_r numeric value label background corner radius ratio. Default 0.1. label_repel Whether repel labels data points. Default FALSE. label_repulsion numeric value repulsion force. Default 20. label_pt_size numeric value label anchor point size. Default 1. label_pt_color character string label anchor point color. Default "black". label_segment_color character string label connector line color. Default "black". label_insitu Whether place raw group names labels instead numeric indices. Default FALSE. label_pos character string function specifying label positions computed per group. Options: "median" (default), "mean", "center", "first", "last", "random", "min", "max", custom function takes numeric vector returns single value. graph Graph/network edge data SpatPointsPlot. Can square adjacency matrix row/column names matching data, data.frame edge coordinates (see graph_x etc.), column name index, "@graph" (extracts data attribute named "graph"). Default NULL. graph_x character string x start coordinate column graph data. graph_y character string y start coordinate column graph data. graph_xend character string x end coordinate column graph data. graph_yend character string y end coordinate column graph data. graph_value character string edge weight column graph data. edge_size numeric vector length 2 specifying line width range graph edges. Default c(0.05, 0.5). edge_alpha numeric value graph edge transparency. Default 0.1. edge_color character string graph edge color. Default "grey40".

title: Spatial visualization functions for terra objects and point data — SpatImagePlot
text: ggplot object height width attributes return_layer = FALSE (default). return_layer = TRUE, list ggplot layers "scales" attribute returned. faceted via multiple fill_by color_by columns, faceted ggplot returned.

title: Spatial visualization functions for terra objects and point data — SpatImagePlot
text: SpatImagePlot Render SpatRaster raster image. Supports single-layer continuous values (gradient fill) 3-channel RGB data (automatic color identity scaling). SpatMasksPlot Render SpatRaster categorical mask overlay. Zero-valued cells treated transparent background; optional polygon borders can added around mask regions. SpatShapesPlot Render spatial shapes (polygons) SpatVector data frame vertex coordinates. Supports single multi-column fill mapping automatic faceting. SpatPointsPlot Render spatial points data frame support color/size/fill mapping, hex binning, rasterized rendering, network/graph overlays, labels, point highlighting.

title: Spatial visualization functions for terra objects and point data — SpatImagePlot
text: Extent cropping — ext provided, SpatRaster cropped via terra::crop(). error raised cells remain within extent. Auto-rasterization — raster exceeds 1e6 cells (raster = TRUE), raster aggregated via terra::aggregate() target resolution raster_dpi. Y-axis flipping — flip_y = TRUE, raster flipped vertically via .flip_y() y-extent negated axis labels can displayed reversed sign. RGB detection — raster exactly 3 layers, treated red/green/blue channels: channel rescaled 0–255 combined hex color via rgb(). scale_fill_identity() used legend guide. Single-layer rendering — otherwise, raster converted x/y/value data frame rendered via geom_raster() scale_fill_gradientn(). Layer return full assembly — return_layer = TRUE, list layers ("scales" attribute set "fill") returned. Otherwise, .wrap_spatial_layers() assembles complete ggplot coord_sf(expand = 0), theme, labels, legend, dimension attributes.

title: Spatial visualization functions for terra objects and point data — SpatImagePlot
text: Extent cropping — ext provided, SpatRaster cropped via terra::crop(). Y-axis flipping — flip_y = TRUE, raster flipped vertically y-extent negated. Background masking — cells value 0 set NA render transparent via na.value = "transparent". Raster layer — mask converted x/y/value data frame rendered via geom_raster() gradient fill scale. Optional borders — add_border = TRUE, mask values polygonized via terra::.polygons(), converted sf, overlaid unfilled geom_sf() specified border_color, border_size, border_alpha. Layer return full assembly — return_layer = TRUE, layers returned; otherwise, .wrap_spatial_layers() creates complete ggplot.

title: Spatial visualization functions for terra objects and point data — SpatImagePlot
text: Column resolution — x y coordinates resolved data, auto-detecting common column names ("x", "X", "sdimx", etc. x; "y", "Y", "sdimy", etc. y) explicitly provided. Extent cropping — ext provided, rows outside extent filtered. Y-axis flipping — flip_y = TRUE, y coordinate column negated via .flip_y(). Multi-column faceting — color_by multiple columns (numeric), data reshaped long format .facet_var column faceted via facet_plot(). Cutoff winsorization — numeric color_by, values outside [lower_cutoff, upper_cutoff] (derived quantiles explicit values) clamped nearest cutoff. Graph / network edges — graph provided, edges resolved adjacency matrix, data.frame, data attribute, rendered geom_segment() segments line width proportional edge weight. Main point layer — one three rendering modes: Regular (hex = FALSE, raster = FALSE) — geom_point() shape, size, color, fill aesthetic mappings. Shapes 21–25 support separate fill border colors. Hex (hex = TRUE) — geom_hex() stat_summary_hex() binned aggregation numeric color_by values. Raster (raster = TRUE) — scattermore::geom_scattermore() efficient rendering large datasets (>1e6 rows). Highlight — highlight specified, highlighted points overlaid larger, colored markers using geom_point() scattermore::geom_scattermore(). Labels — label = TRUE color_by categorical column, group centroid positions computed via aggregate() label_pos function, labels rendered via ggrepel::geom_text_repel() optional background styling repulsion. Layer return full assembly — return_layer = TRUE, layers returned; otherwise, .wrap_spatial_layers() creates complete ggplot facet_plot() applied multi-column faceting active.

title: Spatial visualization functions for terra objects and point data — SpatImagePlot
code: # \donttest{ set.seed(8525) # --- SpatImagePlot --- # Generate a sample SpatRaster r <- terra::rast(     nrows = 50, ncols = 40, vals = runif(2000),     xmin = 0, xmax = 40, ymin = 0, ymax = 50,     crs = "" ) SpatImagePlot(r)  SpatImagePlot(r, raster = TRUE, raster_dpi = 20)  SpatImagePlot(r, alpha = 0.5, theme = "theme_blank",     theme_args = list(add_coord = FALSE), fill_name = "value")  SpatImagePlot(r, ext = c(0, 10, 0, 10), flip_y = FALSE, palette = "viridis")   # --- SpatMasksPlot --- m <- terra::rast(    nrows = 50, ncols = 40,    vals = sample(c(1:5, NA), 2000, replace = TRUE, prob = c(rep(0.04, 5), 0.8)),    xmin = 0, xmax = 40, ymin = 0, ymax = 50,    crs = "" ) SpatMasksPlot(m, border_color = "red")  SpatMasksPlot(m, ext = c(0, 15, 0, 20), add_border = FALSE,     palreverse = TRUE, fill_name = "value") #> Warning: Raster pixels are placed at uneven horizontal intervals and will be shifted #> ℹ Consider using `geom_tile()` instead.   # --- SpatShapesPlot --- polygons <- data.frame(    id = paste0("poly_", 1:10),    cat = sample(LETTERS[1:3], 10, replace = TRUE),    feat1 = rnorm(10),    feat2 = rnorm(10),    geometry = c(        'POLYGON((64.6 75.3,66.0 70.5,66.4 70.2,67.0 69.8,72.8 70.4,64.6 75.3))',        'POLYGON((56.7 63.0,52.3 65.6,48.0 63.2,51.2 55.7,57.1 59.2,56.7 63.0))',        'POLYGON((9.9 16.5,9.3 15.9,8.0 13.1,11.5 7.8,17.8 11.3,9.9 16.5))',        'POLYGON((64.9 37.2,60.3 37.4,57.6 31.7,58.9 29.3,64.0 28.1,64.9 37.2))',        'POLYGON((30.5 49.1,22.4 46.5,22.4 43.9,30.9 41.9,31.6 42.9,30.5 49.1))',        'POLYGON((78.3 57.8,70.5 61.6,71.6 52.7,72.2 52.5,77.4 54.5,78.3 57.8))',        'POLYGON((41.8 23.8,41.3 25.9,41.0 26.4,36.5 28.7,35.8 28.6,41.8 23.8))',        'POLYGON((15.7 75.9,14.2 74.4,15.7 67.5,23.0 69.8,23.4 71.7,15.7 75.9))',        'POLYGON((80.7 37.4,75.3 31.3,77.1 28.5,82.5 28.0,83.1 28.5,80.7 37.4))',        'POLYGON((15.5 37.8,14.4 38.6,7.3 32.6,8.3 30.9,15.1 30.2,15.5 37.8))'    ) )  polygons <- terra::vect(polygons, crs = "EPSG:4326", geom = "geometry")  SpatShapesPlot(polygons)  SpatShapesPlot(polygons, ext = c(0, 20, 0, 20))  SpatShapesPlot(polygons, highlight = 'cat == "A"', highlight_color = "red2")  SpatShapesPlot(polygons, border_color = "red", border_size = 2)  SpatShapesPlot(polygons, fill_by = "cat", fill_name = "category")  # Let border color be determined by fill SpatShapesPlot(polygons, fill_by = "cat", alpha = 0.6, border_color = TRUE)  SpatShapesPlot(polygons, fill_by = "feat1")  SpatShapesPlot(polygons, fill_by = c("feat1", "feat2"), palette = "RdYlBu")   # --- SpatPointsPlot --- # create some random points in the above polygons points <- data.frame(   id = paste0("point_", 1:30),   gene = sample(LETTERS[1:3], 30, replace = TRUE),   feat1 = runif(30, 0, 100),   feat2 = runif(30, 0, 100),   size = runif(30, 1, 5),   x = c(     61.6, 14.3, 12.7, 49.6, 74.9, 58.9, 13.9, 24.7, 16.9, 15.6,     72.4, 60.1, 75.4, 14.9, 80.3, 78.8, 16.7, 27.6, 48.9, 52.5,     12.9, 11.8, 50.4, 25.6, 10.4, 51.9, 73.4, 26.8, 50.4, 60.0   ),   y = c(     32.1, 12.8, 33.2, 59.9, 57.8, 31.9, 10.1, 46.8, 75.3, 69.0,     60.0, 29.4, 54.2, 34.2, 35.3, 33.1, 74.7, 48.0, 63.2, 59.2,     9.2, 15.1, 64.5, 47.1, 11.4, 60.1, 54.1, 44.5, 61.9, 30.3   ) )  SpatPointsPlot(points)  SpatPointsPlot(points, color_by = "gene", size_by = "size", shape = 22,   border_size = 1)  SpatPointsPlot(points, raster = TRUE, raster_dpi = 30, color_by = "feat1")  SpatPointsPlot(points, color_by = c("feat1", "feat2"), size_by = "size")  SpatPointsPlot(points, color_by = "feat1", upper_cutoff = 50)  SpatPointsPlot(points, color_by = "feat1", hex = TRUE)  SpatPointsPlot(points, color_by = "gene", label = TRUE)  SpatPointsPlot(points, color_by = "gene", highlight = 1:20,   highlight_color = "red2", highlight_stroke = 0.8)   # --- Graph/Network functionality --- # Create a simple adjacency matrix for demonstration set.seed(8525) graph_mat <- matrix(0, nrow = 30, ncol = 30) # Add some random connections with weights for(i in 1:30) {   neighbors <- sample(setdiff(1:30, i), size = sample(2:5, 1))   graph_mat[i, neighbors] <- runif(length(neighbors), 0.1, 1) } rownames(graph_mat) <- colnames(graph_mat) <- rownames(points) attr(points, "graph") <- graph_mat  SpatPointsPlot(points, color_by = "gene", graph = "@graph",   edge_color = "grey60", edge_alpha = 0.3)  SpatPointsPlot(points, color_by = "feat1", graph = graph_mat,   edge_size = c(0.1, 1), edge_alpha = 0.5)   # --- Use the `return_layer` argument to get the ggplot layers ext = c(0, 40, 0, 50) ggplot2::ggplot() +   SpatImagePlot(r, return_layer = TRUE, alpha = 0.2, ext = ext) +   SpatShapesPlot(polygons, return_layer = TRUE, ext = ext, fill_by = "white") +   SpatPointsPlot(points, return_layer = TRUE, ext = ext, color_by = "feat1") +   theme_box() +   ggplot2::coord_sf(expand = 0) +   ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(labels = function(x) -x)   # }

title: Validate common arguments — validate_common_args
text: seed random seed use. Default 8525. facet_by character string specifying column name data frame facet plot. Otherwise, data split split_by generate multiple plots combine one using patchwork::wrap_plots split_by column(s) split data plot separately. split_by_sep separator multiple split_by columns. See split_by group_by Columns group data plotting plotting functions support multiple groups, concatenated one column, using group_by_sep separator group_by_sep separator multiple group_by columns. See group_by facet_scales Whether scale axes facets. Default "fixed" options "free", "free_x", "free_y". See ggplot2::facet_wrap facet_nrow numeric value specifying number rows facet. facet_by single column facet_wrap used. facet_ncol numeric value specifying number columns facet. facet_by single column facet_wrap used. facet_byrow logical value indicating whether fill plots row. Default TRUE. theme character string theme class (.e. ggplot2::theme_classic) specifying theme use. Default "theme_this". theme_args list arguments pass theme function. palette character string specifying palette use. named list vector can used specify palettes different split_by values. palcolor character string specifying color use palette. named list can used specify colors different split_by values. values missing, values palette used (palcolor NULL values). expand values expand x y axes. like CSS padding. single value provided, used axes sides. two values provided, first value used top/bottom side second value used left/right side. three values provided, first value used top side, second value used left/right side, third value used bottom side. four values provided, values used top, right, bottom, left sides, respectively. can also use named vector specify values side. axis discrete, values applied 'add' 'expansion' function. axis continuous, values applied 'mult' 'expansion' function. See also https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/expansion.html keep_na logical value character replace NA values data. can also take named list specify different behavior different columns. TRUE NA, NA values replaced NA. FALSE, NA values removed data plotting. character string provided, NA values replaced provided string. named vector/list provided, names column names apply behavior , values one TRUE, FALSE, character string. Without named vector/list, behavior applies categorical/character columns used plot, example, x, group_by, fill_by, etc. keep_empty One FALSE, TRUE "level". can also take named list specify different behavior different columns. Without named list, behavior applies categorical/character columns used plot, example, x, group_by, fill_by, etc. FALSE (default): Drop empty factor levels data plotting. TRUE: Keep empty factor levels show separate category plot. "level": Keep empty factor levels, show plot. assigned colors palette maintain consistency across multiple plots. Alias: levels alpha numeric value specifying transparency plot. x_text_angle numeric value specifying angle x-axis text. aspect.ratio numeric value specifying aspect ratio plot. legend.position character string specifying position legend. waiver(), single groups, legend "none", otherwise "right". legend.direction character string specifying direction legend. title character string specifying title plot. function can used generate title based default title. useful split_by used title needs dynamic. subtitle character string specifying subtitle plot. xlab character string specifying x-axis label. ylab character string specifying y-axis label. combine Whether combine plots one facet FALSE. Default TRUE. nrow numeric value specifying number rows facet. ncol numeric value specifying number columns facet. byrow logical value indicating whether fill plots row. ... Additional arguments.
